commands.tooltip: Starts a container that was previously stopped.

commands.desc: Restart a running or stopped container
commands.tooltip: Stops and then starts a container.

commands.desc: Remove a stopped container
commands.tooltip: Deletes a stopped container. You can't remove a running container without -f.

commands.desc: Force remove a running container
commands.tooltip: Force-stops and removes a running container in one step.

commands.desc: Remove all stopped containers
commands.tooltip: Removes all stopped containers in one command. docker ps -aq lists all container IDs.

commands.desc: Open an interactive bash shell in a running container
commands.tooltip: Opens an interactive bash shell inside a running container — like SSH-ing into it.

commands.desc: Run a single command in a running container
commands.tooltip: Runs a single command inside a running container without opening an interactive shell.

commands.desc: View the logs of a container
commands.tooltip: Shows the output (stdout/stderr) that the container has produced since it started.

commands.desc: Follow container logs in real-time
commands.tooltip: Follows the container's logs in real time, showing new output as it appears. Press Ctrl+C to stop.

commands.desc: View the last 100 lines of container logs
commands.tooltip: Shows only the most recent 100 log lines from the container.

commands.desc: Display detailed JSON information about a container
commands.tooltip: Returns detailed JSON information about a container: its configuration, network settings, volumes, etc.

commands.desc: Extract specific field from container inspect
commands.tooltip: Uses a Go template to extract a specific piece of information from docker inspect output.

commands.desc: List all Docker networks
commands.tooltip: Lists all Docker networks on this host. Networks let containers communicate with each other.

commands.desc: Create a custom Docker network
commands.tooltip: Creates a new custom bridge network that containers can be connected to.

commands.desc: Connect a container to a network
commands.tooltip: Connects an already-running container to an additional network.

commands.desc: List all Docker volumes
commands.tooltip: Lists all Docker volumes — named persistent storage units that survive container restarts.

commands.desc: Create a named Docker volume
commands.tooltip: Creates a named volume. Unlike bind mounts, Docker manages where the data is stored.

commands.desc: Remove a Docker volume
commands.tooltip: Deletes a named volume. Any data stored in it is permanently gone.

commands.desc: Create and start all services defined in docker-compose.yml
commands.tooltip: Starts all services defined in docker-compose.yml — creates containers, networks, and volumes as needed.

commands.desc: Start all services in detached (background) mode
commands.tooltip: Starts all compose services in detached mode — they run in the background.

commands.desc: Stop and remove compose containers, networks
commands.tooltip: Stops and removes all containers and networks created by docker compose. Volumes are preserved.

commands.desc: Stop, remove containers and also delete volumes
commands.tooltip: Stops and removes containers, networks, AND all named volumes — your data will be deleted.

commands.desc: Build or rebuild service images
commands.tooltip: Rebuilds the Docker images defined in the compose file (runs the Dockerfile again).

commands.desc: Follow logs from all compose services
commands.tooltip: Follows the combined log output from all services defined in the compose file.

commands.desc: Open shell in a running compose service container
commands.tooltip: Opens an interactive shell in the 'web' service container — specifying the service name, not container name.

commands.desc: List status of all compose service containers
commands.tooltip: Lists the status of all containers in the compose project.

commands.desc: Remove all stopped containers, dangling images, unused networks
commands.tooltip: Cleans up stopped containers, dangling images, and unused networks in one command.

commands.desc: Remove all unused images, not just dangling ones
commands.tooltip: More aggressive cleanup — removes ALL unused images, not just dangling ones. Good for reclaiming lots of disk space.

commands.desc: Tag an image with a new name/version
commands.tooltip: Creates a new tag pointing to an existing image — like creating an alias.

commands.desc: Copy a file from a container to the host
commands.tooltip: Copies a file from inside a container to the host machine.

commands.desc: Stream live resource usage stats for all running containers
commands.tooltip: Shows live CPU, memory, and network usage for all running containers — like 'top' for Docker.

commands.desc: Show disk usage by Docker objects (images, containers, volumes)
commands.tooltip: Shows how much disk space Docker is using for images, containers, volumes, and build cache.

commands.desc: Start a new tmux session
commands.tooltip: Starts a new tmux session. tmux is a terminal multiplexer — it lets you have multiple terminal windows in one and keep sessions running after you disconnect.

commands.desc: Start a new named tmux session
commands.tooltip: Starts a new tmux session with a name so you can easily find and reattach to it later.

commands.desc: Attach to the most recent detached session
commands.tooltip: Reconnects to the most recently used tmux session after you've been detached.

commands.desc: Attach to a specific named session
commands.tooltip: Reconnects to a specific named tmux session.

commands.desc: Detach from current session (keeps it running): Prefix+d
commands.tooltip: Detaches from the current tmux session, leaving it running in the background. Your work keeps going even if you close the terminal.

commands.desc: List all active tmux sessions
commands.tooltip: Lists all currently running tmux sessions and their window count.

commands.desc: Kill a specific tmux session
commands.tooltip: Terminates a specific tmux session and all the programs running inside it.

commands.desc: Kill the tmux server and all sessions
commands.tooltip: Shuts down the tmux server entirely, killing all sessions.

commands.desc: Rename a session
commands.tooltip: Renames a session from one name to another.

commands.desc: Switch to a different session
commands.tooltip: Switches to a different session without detaching.

commands.desc: Rename the current session interactively
commands.tooltip: Renames the current tmux session. The 'Prefix' is Ctrl+B by default.

commands.desc: Show interactive list of sessions to switch between
commands.tooltip: Opens a visual list of all sessions to jump between them.

commands.desc: Create a new window in the current session
commands.tooltip: Creates a new window (tab) in the current session. Windows are like tabs in a terminal.

commands.desc: Rename the current window
commands.tooltip: Renames the current window.

commands.desc: Kill the current window (with confirmation)
commands.tooltip: Closes the current window after asking for confirmation.

commands.desc: Move to the next window
commands.tooltip: Moves to the next window in the session.

commands.desc: Move to the previous window
commands.tooltip: Moves to the previous window.

commands.desc: Switch to window by number (0 through 9)
commands.tooltip: Switches directly to a window by its number (0 through 9).

commands.desc: Show interactive list of windows to choose from
commands.tooltip: Opens a visual list of all windows in the current session to choose from.

commands.desc: Find a window by searching for its text content
commands.tooltip: Searches all window content for text and jumps to the matching window.

commands.desc: Split the current pane vertically (side by side)
commands.tooltip: Splits the current pane into two side-by-side panes (left and right). A pane is a subdivision of a window.

commands.desc: Split the current pane horizontally (top and bottom)
commands.tooltip: Splits the current pane into top and bottom panes.

commands.desc: Move focus to the pane in the arrow direction
commands.tooltip: Moves focus to the pane in the direction of the arrow key.

commands.desc: Cycle focus to the next pane
commands.tooltip: Moves focus to the next pane, cycling through all panes in the window.

commands.desc: Toggle focus to the last active pane
commands.tooltip: Toggles focus back to the previously active pane.

commands.desc: Kill the current pane (with confirmation)
commands.tooltip: Closes the current pane after asking for confirmation.

commands.desc: Toggle zoom on current pane (fullscreen/restore)
commands.tooltip: Zooms the current pane to fill the entire window — press again to restore split view.

commands.desc: Cycle through pane layouts (even-horizontal, tiled, etc.)
commands.tooltip: Cycles through different pane arrangement layouts.

commands.desc: Resize current pane in the arrow direction
commands.tooltip: Resizes the current pane by one cell in the arrow key direction.

commands.desc: Move current pane to a new window
commands.tooltip: Moves the current pane into a new separate window.

commands.desc: Show pane numbers briefly (press number to jump)
commands.tooltip: Briefly shows a number on each pane — press the number to jump to that pane.

commands.desc: Enter copy mode to scroll through and copy output
commands.tooltip: Enters copy mode, allowing you to scroll up through the terminal history and copy text.

commands.desc: Paste the most recent tmux buffer
commands.tooltip: Pastes the most recently copied text from the tmux clipboard.

commands.desc: Exit copy mode
commands.tooltip: Exits copy mode and returns to the normal terminal.

commands.desc: Search forward through pane output
commands.tooltip: Searches forward through the scrollback history in copy mode.

commands.desc: Search backward through pane output
commands.tooltip: Searches backward through the scrollback history in copy mode.

commands.desc: Start text selection in copy mode
commands.tooltip: Starts selecting text while in copy mode.

commands.desc: Copy selected text to tmux buffer
commands.tooltip: Copies the selected text into the tmux clipboard buffer.

commands.desc: Reload tmux configuration from file
commands.tooltip: Applies changes from your ~/.tmux.conf configuration file without restarting tmux.

commands.desc: Enable mouse support in tmux from command prompt
commands.tooltip: Enables mouse support so you can click on panes and windows and scroll with the mouse wheel.

commands.desc: Enter insert mode before the cursor position
commands.tooltip: Enters insert mode so you can type text. Vim starts in normal mode where keys are commands, not text.

commands.desc: Enter insert mode after the cursor position
commands.tooltip: Enters insert mode but places the cursor AFTER the current character (versus i which inserts BEFORE).

commands.desc: Enter insert mode at the beginning of the current line
commands.tooltip: Enters insert mode at the very beginning of the current line.

commands.desc: Enter insert mode at the end of the current line
commands.tooltip: Enters insert mode at the very end of the current line.

commands.desc: Open a new line below and enter insert mode
commands.tooltip: Creates a new blank line below the current line and enters insert mode there.

commands.desc: Open a new line above and enter insert mode
commands.tooltip: Creates a new blank line above the current line and enters insert mode there.

commands.desc: Return to normal mode from any other mode
commands.tooltip: Returns from insert, visual, or any other mode back to normal mode. Your safety key in Vim.

commands.desc: Enter visual mode for character-by-character selection
commands.tooltip: Enters visual mode where you can highlight individual characters by moving the cursor.

commands.desc: Enter visual line mode to select whole lines
commands.tooltip: Enters visual line mode, which selects entire lines at a time.

commands.desc: Enter visual block mode for column/rectangular selection
commands.tooltip: Enters visual block mode for rectangular selections — useful for editing multiple lines simultaneously.

commands.desc: Enter command-line (ex) mode
commands.tooltip: Enters the command line at the bottom of Vim where you can type Ex commands like :w (save) or :q (quit).

commands.desc: Move cursor one character to the left
commands.tooltip: Moves the cursor one position to the left. Vim uses h/j/k/l instead of arrow keys for speed.

commands.desc: Move cursor one line down
commands.tooltip: Moves the cursor one line down.

commands.desc: Move cursor one line up
commands.tooltip: Moves the cursor one line up.

commands.desc: Move cursor one character to the right
commands.tooltip: Moves the cursor one position to the right.

commands.desc: Move forward to the start of the next word
commands.tooltip: Jumps the cursor forward to the start of the next word.

commands.desc: Move backward to the start of the previous word
commands.tooltip: Jumps the cursor backward to the start of the previous word.

commands.desc: Move forward to the end of the current/next word
commands.tooltip: Jumps the cursor forward to the end of the current or next word.

commands.desc: Move to the first character of the current line
commands.tooltip: Jumps to the very first character of the current line.

commands.desc: Move to the last character of the current line
commands.tooltip: Jumps to the very last character of the current line.

commands.desc: Move to the first non-whitespace character of the line
commands.tooltip: Jumps to the first non-whitespace (non-space/tab) character of the line.

commands.desc: Move cursor to the very first line of the file
commands.tooltip: Jumps the cursor to the very first line of the file.

commands.desc: Move cursor to the very last line of the file
commands.tooltip: Jumps the cursor to the very last line of the file.

commands.desc: Jump to line 50
commands.tooltip: Jumps directly to line number 50. Useful with :set number to show line numbers.

commands.desc: Scroll forward (down) one full screen
commands.tooltip: Scrolls down by one full screen.

commands.desc: Scroll backward (up) one full screen
commands.tooltip: Scrolls up by one full screen.

commands.desc: Scroll down half a screen
commands.tooltip: Scrolls down by half a screen.

commands.desc: Scroll up half a screen
commands.tooltip: Scrolls up by half a screen.

commands.desc: Set mark 'a' at the current cursor position
commands.tooltip: Sets a named bookmark at the current cursor position. Replace 'a' with any letter.

commands.desc: Jump to the exact position of mark 'a'
commands.tooltip: Jumps to the exact cursor position (column and line) of the bookmark named 'a'.

commands.desc: Jump to the start of the line containing mark 'a'
commands.tooltip: Jumps to the start of the line containing bookmark 'a' (single quote, not backtick).

commands.desc: Delete the character under the cursor
commands.tooltip: Deletes the character under the cursor.

commands.desc: Delete the character before the cursor
commands.tooltip: Deletes the character just before the cursor.

commands.desc: Delete from cursor to the start of the next word
commands.tooltip: Deletes from the cursor position to the start of the next word.

commands.desc: Delete (cut) the entire current line
commands.tooltip: Deletes the entire current line (and copies it to the clipboard for pasting).

commands.desc: Delete from cursor to end of line
commands.tooltip: Deletes from the cursor position to the end of the line.

commands.desc: Delete from cursor to beginning of line
commands.tooltip: Deletes from the cursor position to the beginning of the line.

commands.desc: Delete from cursor to end of line (same as d$)
commands.tooltip: Deletes from the cursor to the end of the line — same as d$.

commands.desc: Yank (copy) the current line
commands.tooltip: Copies (yanks) the current line without deleting it. 'Yank' is Vim's word for copy.

commands.desc: Yank from cursor to start of next word
commands.tooltip: Copies from the cursor to the start of the next word.

commands.desc: Yank from cursor to end of line
commands.tooltip: Copies from the cursor to the end of the line.

commands.desc: Paste yanked or deleted text after cursor/current line
commands.tooltip: Pastes copied or deleted text after the cursor (or below the current line for whole-line yanks).

commands.desc: Paste yanked or deleted text before cursor/current line
commands.tooltip: Pastes copied or deleted text before the cursor (or above the current line for whole-line yanks).

commands.desc: Replace a single character under cursor
commands.tooltip: Replaces the single character under the cursor with the next character you type, then returns to normal mode.

commands.desc: Enter replace mode to overwrite characters
commands.tooltip: Enters replace mode where each character you type overwrites the character under the cursor.

commands.desc: Change (delete and enter insert mode) to next word
commands.tooltip: Deletes to the start of the next word and enters insert mode — 'change word'.

commands.desc: Delete the current line and enter insert mode
commands.tooltip: Deletes the entire current line and enters insert mode — replaces the whole line.

commands.desc: Delete to end of line and enter insert mode
commands.tooltip: Deletes from cursor to end of line and enters insert mode.

commands.desc: Delete character and enter insert mode (substitute)
commands.tooltip: Deletes the character under the cursor and enters insert mode — substitute one character.

commands.desc: Undo the last change
commands.tooltip: Undoes the most recent change. Press repeatedly to undo multiple changes.

commands.desc: Redo the last undone change
commands.tooltip: Redoes a change that was undone with u. Ctrl+r to redo.

commands.desc: Search forward for a pattern
commands.tooltip: Searches forward through the file for the pattern. Press n for the next match.

commands.desc: Search backward for a pattern
commands.tooltip: Searches backward through the file for the pattern. Press N for the previous match.

commands.desc: Jump to the next search match
commands.tooltip: Jumps to the next occurrence of the search pattern.

commands.desc: Jump to the previous search match
commands.tooltip: Jumps to the previous occurrence of the search pattern.

commands.desc: Replace all occurrences of 'old' with 'new' on current line
commands.tooltip: Replaces all occurrences of 'old' with 'new' on the current line only.

commands.desc: Replace all occurrences throughout the entire file
commands.tooltip: Replaces all occurrences of 'old' with 'new' throughout the entire file. % means 'all lines'.

commands.desc: Replace all with confirmation prompt for each
commands.tooltip: Replaces all occurrences but asks for confirmation before each one. c means 'confirm'.

commands.desc: Save (write) the current file
commands.tooltip: Saves (writes) the current file to disk without quitting.

commands.desc: Save to a new filename
commands.tooltip: Saves the current buffer as a new file with the given filename.

commands.desc: Quit vim (fails if unsaved changes)
commands.tooltip: Quits Vim. Fails if there are unsaved changes — use :w first or :q! to force quit.

commands.desc: Force quit without saving changes
commands.tooltip: Quits Vim without saving — discards all changes. The ! overrides the 'unsaved changes' warning.

commands.desc: Save and quit
commands.tooltip: Saves the file and quits in one command.

commands.desc: Save and quit (only writes if changes exist)
commands.tooltip: Like :wq but only writes to disk if the file has actually been modified.

commands.desc: Save and quit (shortcut for :wq)
commands.tooltip: Keyboard shortcut for :wq — saves and quits. ZZ (two capital Z's).

commands.desc: Quit without saving (shortcut for :q!)
commands.tooltip: Keyboard shortcut for :q! — quits without saving. ZQ (capital Z, Q).

commands.desc: Open a file in the current buffer
commands.tooltip: Opens a file in the current Vim buffer, replacing what's currently shown.

commands.desc: Open a file in a horizontal split
commands.tooltip: Opens a file in a new horizontal split — two editing areas stacked top/bottom.

commands.desc: Open a file in a vertical split
commands.tooltip: Opens a file in a new vertical split — two editing areas side by side.

commands.desc: Cycle focus between open splits
commands.tooltip: Moves focus to the next split window, cycling through them.

commands.desc: Move focus to split in the specified direction
commands.tooltip: Moves focus directly to the split in the specified direction (h=left, j=down, k=up, l=right).

commands.desc: Make all splits equal size
commands.tooltip: Resizes all splits to be equal size.

commands.desc: Open a file in a new tab
commands.tooltip: Opens a file in a new tab within Vim. Navigate tabs with gt (next) and gT (previous).

commands.desc: Go to the next tab
commands.tooltip: Moves to the next tab.

commands.desc: Go to the previous tab
commands.tooltip: Moves to the previous tab.

commands.desc: Yank selection into named register 'a'
commands.tooltip: Copies (yanks) the selected text into the named register 'a'. Registers are named clipboards.

commands.desc: Paste from named register 'a'
commands.tooltip: Pastes from the named register 'a' — the content you yanked with \"ay earlier.

commands.desc: Start recording a macro into register 'a'
commands.tooltip: Starts recording a macro — every key you press gets recorded into register 'a'.

commands.desc: Stop recording the macro
commands.tooltip: Stops recording the macro. Now it's stored in register 'a'.

commands.desc: Execute (play back) macro stored in register 'a'
commands.tooltip: Plays back the macro stored in register 'a' — repeats everything you recorded.

commands.desc: Replay the most recently executed macro
commands.tooltip: Replays the most recently executed macro without specifying which register.

commands.desc: Close (fold) the fold at the cursor
commands.tooltip: Closes (folds) a section of code, collapsing it to a single summary line. Requires fold settings.

commands.desc: Open (unfold) the fold at the cursor
commands.tooltip: Opens (unfolds) a folded section of code.

commands.desc: Toggle the fold at the cursor (open/close)
commands.tooltip: Toggles a fold — if it's open it closes, if it's closed it opens.

commands.desc: List all open buffers
commands.tooltip: Lists all currently open buffers (files open in this Vim session).

commands.desc: Switch to the next buffer
commands.tooltip: Switches to the next buffer in the list.

commands.desc: Switch to the previous buffer
commands.tooltip: Switches to the previous buffer.

commands.desc: Delete (close) the current buffer
commands.tooltip: Closes and removes the current buffer from the list.

commands.desc: Initialize a new npm project interactively, creating package.json
commands.tooltip: Creates a package.json file (the project's configuration file) by asking you a series of setup questions.

commands.desc: Initialize npm project with all defaults, no prompts
commands.tooltip: Creates a package.json with all default values, skipping the interactive questions. -y means 'yes to everything'.

commands.desc: Install all dependencies listed in package.json
commands.tooltip: Downloads and installs all packages listed in package.json into the node_modules folder.

commands.desc: Install a package and save to dependencies
commands.tooltip: Downloads and installs a specific package (express) and adds it to package.json's dependencies.

commands.desc: Install a package as a dev dependency
commands.tooltip: Installs a package as a 'development dependency' — only needed during development, not in production. -D means 'dev'.

commands.desc: Install a package globally
commands.tooltip: Installs a package globally so it's available as a command-line tool anywhere on your system, not just in this project.

commands.desc: Install a specific version of a package
commands.tooltip: Installs a specific version of a package — useful for reproducibility.

commands.desc: Remove a package from dependencies
commands.tooltip: Removes a package and deletes it from package.json.

commands.desc: Update all packages to latest compatible versions
commands.tooltip: Updates all installed packages to the latest versions allowed by the version constraints in package.json.

commands.desc: Run the 'build' script defined in package.json
commands.tooltip: Runs the 'build' script from package.json's 'scripts' section — usually compiles or bundles the code.

commands.desc: Run the 'dev' script (commonly starts development server)
commands.tooltip: Runs the 'dev' script — usually starts a development server with hot reloading.

commands.desc: Run the test script from package.json
commands.tooltip: Runs the 'test' script from package.json — executes the project's test suite.

commands.desc: Publish the package to the npm registry
commands.tooltip: Publishes the package to the public npm registry so others can install it.

commands.desc: Create a global symlink for local package development
commands.tooltip: Creates a symlink from the global node_modules to the current package, so you can test it globally as if it were installed.

commands.desc: Check for known security vulnerabilities in dependencies
commands.tooltip: Checks all installed packages for known security vulnerabilities and reports them.

commands.desc: Automatically fix security vulnerabilities where possible
commands.tooltip: Attempts to automatically fix vulnerabilities by updating packages to safe versions.

commands.desc: List installed packages in tree format
commands.tooltip: Shows all installed packages in a tree structure, showing their dependencies.

commands.desc: List only top-level installed packages
commands.tooltip: Shows only the directly installed packages (depth 0), hiding nested dependencies.

commands.desc: List packages that have newer versions available
commands.tooltip: Lists packages that have newer versions available than what's currently installed.

commands.desc: Clear the npm cache
commands.tooltip: Deletes all cached package data that npm stores locally. Useful when you suspect a corrupt cache.

commands.desc: Run a package without installing it globally (npx)
commands.tooltip: Runs a package without installing it permanently. npx downloads it temporarily, runs it, then discards it.

commands.desc: Create a tsconfig.json using npx to run TypeScript compiler
commands.tooltip: Creates a tsconfig.json (TypeScript configuration file) using npx to run the TypeScript compiler.

commands.desc: Add a package to dependencies with Yarn
commands.tooltip: Adds a package to the project using Yarn (an alternative to npm with faster installs).

commands.desc: Add a package as a dev dependency with Yarn
commands.tooltip: Adds a package as a dev dependency with Yarn.

commands.desc: Remove a package with Yarn
commands.tooltip: Removes a package with Yarn.

commands.desc: Upgrade all packages to latest compatible versions
commands.tooltip: Updates all packages to the latest compatible versions with Yarn.

commands.desc: Install Node.js version 20 using nvm
commands.tooltip: Downloads and installs Node.js version 20 using nvm (Node Version Manager).

commands.desc: Switch to Node.js version 20
commands.tooltip: Switches the active Node.js version to 20. Each project can use a different version.

commands.desc: List all installed Node.js versions
commands.tooltip: Lists all Node.js versions you've installed via nvm.

commands.desc: Evaluate a JavaScript expression from the command line
commands.tooltip: Evaluates a JavaScript expression directly from the command line without a file.

commands.desc: Run a Python script
commands.tooltip: Runs a Python script file. python3 specifies Python version 3 (as opposed to python2).

commands.desc: Execute a Python expression from the command line
commands.tooltip: Runs a single Python statement directly without creating a script file. -c means 'command'.

commands.desc: Start a simple HTTP file server on port 8000
commands.tooltip: Starts a simple HTTP server that serves files from the current directory on port 8000. Great for quickly sharing files on a local network.

commands.desc: Pretty-print and validate a JSON file
commands.tooltip: Reads a JSON file, validates it, and prints it with proper indentation — useful for inspecting minified JSON.

commands.desc: Run script and drop into interactive REPL after execution
commands.tooltip: Runs a script and then drops you into the Python interactive prompt (REPL) with all of the script's variables still in scope.

commands.desc: Install a Python package from PyPI
commands.tooltip: Downloads and installs a Python package from PyPI (the Python Package Index) — like an app store for Python libraries.

commands.desc: Install a specific version of a package
commands.tooltip: Installs a specific version of a package. == means exact version match.

commands.desc: Install all packages listed in a requirements file
commands.tooltip: Reads a requirements.txt file (a list of packages) and installs all of them.

commands.desc: Uninstall a Python package
commands.tooltip: Removes an installed Python package.

commands.desc: List all installed Python packages
commands.tooltip: Shows all Python packages currently installed in the active environment.

commands.desc: List installed packages with available upgrades
commands.tooltip: Lists packages that have newer versions available on PyPI.

commands.desc: Output all installed packages in requirements format
commands.tooltip: Outputs all installed packages in requirements.txt format — the format used to reproduce an environment.

commands.desc: Save current environment packages to requirements file
commands.tooltip: Saves the full list of installed packages (with versions) to requirements.txt for sharing or reproduction.

commands.desc: Show detailed info about an installed package
commands.tooltip: Shows detailed information about an installed package: version, location, dependencies.

commands.desc: Upgrade pip itself to the latest version
commands.tooltip: Updates pip (the package installer) itself to the latest version.

commands.desc: Create a virtual environment in a 'venv' directory
commands.tooltip: Creates an isolated Python environment in a 'venv' folder. A virtual environment keeps your project's packages separate from other projects.

commands.desc: Activate the virtual environment on Linux/Mac
commands.tooltip: Activates the virtual environment. After this, Python and pip commands use the venv's packages instead of the system ones.

commands.desc: Deactivate the current virtual environment
commands.tooltip: Deactivates the active virtual environment and returns to using the system Python.

commands.desc: Create a virtual environment using virtualenv tool
commands.tooltip: Creates a virtual environment using the virtualenv tool, which has more features than the built-in venv.

commands.desc: Install the current package in editable/development mode
commands.tooltip: Installs the current directory as a package in 'editable' mode — changes to your source code take effect immediately without reinstalling.

commands.desc: Install package using the python3-specific pip module
commands.tooltip: Installs a package using Python 3's pip specifically, useful when you have multiple Python versions.

commands.desc: Create a conda virtual environment with Python 3.11
commands.tooltip: Creates a conda virtual environment with a specific Python version. conda manages both packages and environments.

commands.desc: Activate a conda environment
commands.tooltip: Activates a conda environment — switches your Python and packages to those in 'myenv'.

commands.desc: Install a package using conda
commands.tooltip: Installs a package using conda's package manager instead of pip.

commands.desc: List all conda environments
commands.tooltip: Lists all conda environments you've created.

commands.desc: Shebang line to use bash found in environment PATH
commands.tooltip: The first line of a bash script that tells the OS to run it with bash. /usr/bin/env finds bash anywhere in the PATH.

commands.desc: Shebang line to use bash at the fixed path /bin/bash
commands.tooltip: Tells the OS to run the script with bash at the fixed location /bin/bash. Less portable than env bash.

commands.desc: Assign a value to a variable (no spaces around =)
commands.tooltip: Assigns a value to a variable. No spaces around the = sign — spaces would break this in bash.

commands.desc: Expand and print a variable's value
commands.tooltip: Prints the value of a variable. The $ expands (substitutes) the variable's value.

commands.desc: Double-quoted string with variable expansion
commands.tooltip: Prints a string with a variable's value substituted inside. Double quotes allow variable expansion; single quotes don't.

commands.desc: Declare an indexed array with three elements
commands.tooltip: Creates a list (array) with three elements. Access them with ${arr[0]}, ${arr[1]}, ${arr[2]}.

commands.desc: Access an array element by index (zero-based)
commands.tooltip: Accesses the element at index 1 (the second element, since arrays start at 0).

commands.desc: Expand all elements of an array
commands.tooltip: Expands (prints) all elements of the array, space-separated.

commands.desc: Get the number of elements in an array
commands.tooltip: Prints the number of elements in the array. The # prefix means 'length of'.

commands.cmd: for i in ${arr[@]}; do echo $i; done
commands.desc: Loop over all elements of an array
commands.tooltip: Loops over every element in the array and runs a command for each one.

commands.cmd: for i in $(seq 1 10); do echo $i; done
commands.desc: Loop from 1 to 10 using seq
commands.tooltip: Loops from 1 to 10 using seq to generate the numbers, running a command for each number.

commands.cmd: for file in *.txt; do echo $file; done
commands.desc: Loop over all .txt files in current directory
commands.tooltip: Loops over every .txt file in the current directory, running a command for each.

commands.cmd: while read line; do echo $line; done < file.txt
commands.desc: Read a file line by line with a while loop
commands.tooltip: Reads a file one line at a time and processes each line in the loop body.

commands.cmd: while true; do command; sleep 5; done
commands.desc: Infinite loop running a command every 5 seconds
commands.tooltip: Runs a command every 5 seconds forever. Press Ctrl+C to stop. Useful for polling or monitoring.

commands.desc: Basic if statement syntax
commands.tooltip: Basic if statement structure in bash. The condition goes inside [ ] and fi closes the if block.

commands.desc: If statement using double brackets (preferred)
commands.tooltip: Preferred if syntax using [[ ]] which handles more cases safely, like strings with spaces.

commands.desc: Check if a file exists and is a regular file
commands.tooltip: Checks if a file exists and is a regular file (not a directory or link). -f is the 'file exists' test.

commands.desc: Check if a path exists and is a directory
commands.tooltip: Checks if a path exists and is a directory. -d is the 'directory exists' test.

commands.desc: Check if a variable is empty (zero length)
commands.tooltip: Checks if a variable is empty (zero length). -z means 'zero length string'.

commands.desc: Check if a variable is non-empty
commands.tooltip: Checks if a variable has a value (is non-empty). -n means 'non-zero length string'.

commands.desc: Compare numbers: greater than
commands.tooltip: Compares two numbers: -gt means 'greater than'. For strings use == or !=.

commands.desc: Case statement for matching variable against patterns
commands.tooltip: A case statement matches a variable against multiple patterns — cleaner than many if/elif/else chains.

commands.desc: Define a reusable function
commands.tooltip: Defines a reusable function. $1 inside the function refers to the first argument passed to it.

commands.desc: Call a function with an argument
commands.tooltip: Calls the function named 'greet' and passes 'Alice' as its first argument ($1).

commands.desc: Capture the output of a command in a variable
commands.tooltip: Runs a command and captures its output (what it would print to the screen) into a variable.

commands.desc: Perform arithmetic and store result
commands.tooltip: Performs arithmetic. $(( )) is bash's arithmetic expansion; the result is stored in NUM.

commands.desc: Calculate modulo using arithmetic expansion
commands.tooltip: Calculates the remainder (modulo) of 10 ÷ 3 = 1, using arithmetic expansion.

commands.desc: Short-circuit AND: run second command only if first succeeds
commands.tooltip: Short-circuit AND: run the second command only if the first succeeds (exits with 0). Like 'if A then do B'.

commands.desc: Short-circuit OR: run second command only if first fails
commands.tooltip: Short-circuit OR: run the second command only if the first fails. Like 'try A, if it fails then do B'.

commands.desc: Strict mode: exit on error, unset variable, or pipe failure
commands.tooltip: Enables strict mode for safer scripts: exit on any error, treat unset variables as errors, and fail pipelines on any step's failure.

commands.desc: View the bash run-commands file for interactive shells
commands.tooltip: Shows your main bash configuration file, which runs every time you open a new terminal. It's where aliases, PATH changes, and settings live.

commands.desc: Edit the bash configuration file
commands.tooltip: Opens your .bashrc file for editing so you can add aliases, change settings, or modify PATH.

commands.desc: Apply changes to .bashrc in the current shell session
commands.tooltip: Reloads your .bashrc so changes you just made take effect in the current terminal without reopening it.

commands.desc: View login shell configuration file
commands.tooltip: Shows your login shell config — runs when you log in (not for every new terminal). Less frequently edited than .bashrc.

commands.desc: View the shell-agnostic login profile file
commands.tooltip: Shows the general login profile for any POSIX shell (not just bash). Runs at login.

commands.desc: View the dedicated aliases file (sourced by .bashrc)
commands.tooltip: Shows a dedicated file for storing aliases, usually sourced by .bashrc.

commands.desc: Add a directory to the PATH for the current session
commands.tooltip: Adds a new directory to PATH for the current session. Programs in /opt/myapp/bin can now be run by name.

commands.desc: Print the current PATH variable showing all directories
commands.tooltip: Shows all directories the shell searches when you type a command name.

commands.desc: Print the current user's home directory path
commands.tooltip: Shows your home directory path — where your personal files and settings are stored.

commands.desc: Print the current user's default shell
commands.tooltip: Shows which shell program you're using (e.g. /bin/bash or /bin/zsh).

commands.desc: Print the current username
commands.tooltip: Shows your current username.

commands.desc: Print the current locale/language setting
commands.tooltip: Shows your locale setting — which language and character encoding the system uses.

commands.desc: Print all environment variables
commands.tooltip: Prints all environment variables, including those that programs inherit when launched from this shell.

commands.desc: Print the value of a specific environment variable
commands.tooltip: Prints the value of a single specific environment variable.

commands.desc: Display all environment variables (similar to printenv)
commands.tooltip: Prints all environment variables — functionally similar to printenv with no arguments.

commands.desc: Replace $VAR placeholders in a file with actual env values
commands.tooltip: Reads a template file and replaces $VAR placeholders with their actual values from the environment.

commands.desc: Allow direnv to load the .envrc file in current directory
commands.tooltip: Tells direnv to trust and activate the .envrc file in the current directory. direnv auto-loads variables when you enter directories.

commands.desc: Edit the .envrc file for current directory with direnv
commands.tooltip: Opens the .envrc file for editing using direnv's built-in editor with change detection.

commands.desc: View a dotenv file containing environment variable definitions
commands.tooltip: Shows a .env file which typically contains key=value pairs for environment variables used by an app.

commands.desc: Load variables from a .env file into the current shell
commands.tooltip: Reads all key=value pairs from a .env file and exports them as environment variables in the current shell.

commands.desc: Open a file with the default desktop application
commands.tooltip: Opens a file with whichever application is registered as the default handler for that file type (like double-clicking in a GUI).

commands.desc: Launch fuzzy finder to search from stdin interactively
commands.tooltip: Opens fzf, a fuzzy finder that lets you type parts of a string to filter and select from a list. Type anywhere in the string — no need for exact matches.

commands.desc: Fuzzy find files with a live preview pane
commands.tooltip: Runs fzf for file selection and shows a live file preview on the right side as you move through matches.

commands.desc: Open a fzf-selected file in vim
commands.tooltip: Combines fzf and vim: fzf lets you pick a file interactively, then opens it in vim.

commands.desc: Fuzzy search through command history
commands.tooltip: Lets you fuzzy search through your command history and re-run a past command.

commands.desc: Search current directory recursively with ripgrep (fast grep)
commands.tooltip: Searches the current directory tree for a pattern using ripgrep (rg) — much faster than grep for large codebases.

commands.desc: Show only filenames with matches (ripgrep)
commands.tooltip: Shows only filenames that contain matches, without the matching lines. -l means 'list files'.

commands.desc: Show matches with line numbers (ripgrep)
commands.tooltip: Shows matches with their line numbers. -n means 'line numbers'.

commands.desc: Search only Python files with ripgrep
commands.tooltip: Searches only Python (.py) files, ignoring all other file types.

commands.desc: Case-insensitive search with ripgrep
commands.tooltip: Case-insensitive ripgrep search. -i means 'ignore case'.

commands.desc: Include hidden files in ripgrep search
commands.tooltip: Includes hidden files and directories (those starting with .) in the ripgrep search.

commands.desc: Find files by name pattern using fd (faster than find)
commands.tooltip: Searches for files by name using fd — a faster, more user-friendly alternative to find.

commands.desc: Find all Python files using fd
commands.tooltip: Finds all Python files using fd.

commands.desc: Find directories named 'test' using fd
commands.tooltip: Searches for directories (not files) named 'test' with fd.

commands.desc: Include hidden files in fd search
commands.tooltip: Includes hidden files in fd's search.

commands.desc: View file with syntax highlighting (cat replacement)
commands.tooltip: Shows a file with syntax highlighting, line numbers, and a styled border using bat. A more readable alternative to cat.

commands.desc: List all languages that bat supports for highlighting
commands.tooltip: Lists all languages bat knows how to syntax-highlight.

commands.desc: List files with colors and icons (modern ls replacement)
commands.tooltip: Lists files in long format with icons, colors, and Git status indicators using exa — a modern ls replacement.

commands.desc: List files with icons and colors (another ls replacement)
commands.tooltip: Similar to exa — lists files with icons and colors using lsd (another modern ls replacement).

commands.desc: Show practical examples for a command (simpler than man)
commands.tooltip: Shows practical real-world examples of the tar command, much more beginner-friendly than the manual page.

commands.desc: Show tldr examples for the find command
commands.tooltip: Shows tldr examples for the find command.

commands.desc: Open the full manual page for a command
commands.tooltip: Opens the full technical manual page for a command. Press q to quit, / to search.

commands.desc: Search manual pages for a keyword (same as apropos)
commands.tooltip: Searches manual page names and descriptions for a keyword. -k means 'keyword search'.

commands.desc: Read the full info documentation for a command
commands.tooltip: Opens the info documentation for a command — often more complete than man pages.

commands.desc: Search all man page descriptions for a keyword
commands.tooltip: Searches all man page short descriptions for a keyword. Same as man -k.

commands.desc: Quickly find a file by name using the locate database
commands.tooltip: Finds files by name using a pre-built database. Much faster than find for simple name lookups.

commands.desc: Update the locate/mlocate database for fast searches
commands.tooltip: Rebuilds the locate database by scanning the filesystem. Run after creating new files.

commands.desc: Jump to a frequently visited directory matching 'myproject' (zoxide/z)
commands.tooltip: Jumps to a frequently visited directory by fuzzy matching its name. Works with zoxide or the z tool.

commands.desc: Jump to a commonly visited directory by partial name
commands.tooltip: Jumps to a recently visited directory matching a partial name. Uses the autojump database of your history.

commands.desc: Suggest and fix the previous mistyped command automatically
commands.tooltip: Analyzes your last failed command and suggests a corrected version — then runs it if you approve.

commands.desc: Suspend the current process to background, then bring back
commands.tooltip: Ctrl+Z suspends the current process (pauses it). 'fg' brings it back to the foreground. Like pausing and resuming a program.

commands.desc: Run Alpine container interactively and auto-remove it on exit
commands.tooltip: Runs an Alpine Linux container (minimal, ~5MB) for interactive exploration. --rm deletes the container automatically when you exit.

commands.desc: Run nginx in detached mode with a custom container name
commands.tooltip: Starts a container named 'myapp' running in the background. -d means detached (runs without occupying your terminal).

commands.desc: Set the container's hostname to myhost
commands.tooltip: Sets the container's internal hostname to 'myhost'. Programs inside the container see this as their hostname.

commands.desc: Load environment variables into a container from a .env file
commands.tooltip: Reads all KEY=VALUE lines from a .env file and injects them as environment variables inside the container.

commands.desc: Set a single environment variable inside the container
commands.tooltip: Sets a single environment variable inside the container. Programs inside can read NODE_ENV to get 'production'.

commands.desc: Auto-restart container unless manually stopped (survives reboots)
commands.tooltip: Configures the container to automatically restart if it stops, but NOT if you manually stop it with docker stop.

commands.desc: Always restart the container automatically, including on Docker daemon restart
commands.tooltip: Configures the container to always restart automatically, including after a Docker daemon or system restart.

commands.desc: Restart container on failure, max 3 retries
commands.tooltip: Restarts the container on failure, but only up to 3 times before giving up.

commands.desc: Limit container RAM usage to 512 MB
commands.tooltip: Limits the container to 512 MB of RAM. If it tries to use more, the OS will kill it.

commands.desc: Limit container to 1.5 CPU cores
commands.tooltip: Restricts the container to using at most 1.5 CPU cores, leaving other processes unaffected.

commands.desc: Set total memory + swap limit to 1 GB
commands.tooltip: Sets the combined RAM + swap limit to 1 GB. Swap is disk space used as extra RAM when real RAM is full.

commands.desc: Set relative CPU weight (default 1024) for scheduling priority
commands.tooltip: Sets a relative CPU weight. Default is 1024; setting 512 means this container gets half the CPU time of default-weight containers when CPU is contested.

commands.desc: Limit the number of processes the container can spawn to 100
commands.tooltip: Limits the number of processes the container can spawn to 100, preventing fork bombs.

commands.desc: Mount the container's root filesystem as read-only
commands.tooltip: Makes the container's root filesystem read-only — files inside can't be changed. Useful for security.

commands.desc: Mount a tmpfs (in-memory) filesystem at /tmp inside the container
commands.tooltip: Creates a temporary in-memory filesystem at /tmp inside the container. Fast, but data is lost when the container stops.

commands.desc: Run container process as UID 1000, GID 1000 (non-root)
commands.tooltip: Runs the container process as a specific non-root user (UID 1000, GID 1000). Reduces security risk compared to running as root.

commands.desc: Set the working directory inside the container to /app
commands.tooltip: Sets the starting directory for commands run inside the container to /app.

commands.desc: Override the image's ENTRYPOINT with /bin/sh
commands.tooltip: Overrides the default ENTRYPOINT (the main command the container runs) with /bin/sh for debugging.

commands.desc: Use the host network stack directly (no network isolation)
commands.tooltip: Bypasses Docker's network namespace isolation — the container shares the host's network interfaces directly.

commands.desc: Disable all networking for the container
commands.tooltip: Completely disables networking for the container — it can't communicate with the host or other containers.

commands.desc: Add a custom /etc/hosts entry inside the container
commands.tooltip: Adds a custom entry to the container's /etc/hosts file, mapping a hostname to an IP address.

commands.desc: Set a custom DNS server for the container
commands.tooltip: Sets the DNS server the container uses for name resolution, instead of inheriting from the host.

commands.desc: Auto-publish all EXPOSE'd ports to random host ports
commands.tooltip: Automatically maps all ports listed in EXPOSE in the Dockerfile to random available host ports.

commands.desc: Legacy: link a container to another by alias (use networks instead)
commands.tooltip: Legacy: creates a network connection between containers. Modern practice uses custom bridge networks instead.

commands.desc: Add a Linux capability (e.g. SYS_PTRACE for debugging)
commands.tooltip: Grants a specific Linux kernel capability to the container. SYS_PTRACE enables debugging with tools like strace.

commands.desc: Drop all Linux capabilities for a hardened container
commands.tooltip: Removes all Linux capabilities for maximum security hardening. Grants nothing; you add back only what's needed.

commands.desc: Run container with full host privileges (use with extreme caution)
commands.tooltip: Gives the container full root-level access to the host system. Extremely dangerous — only for trusted code.

commands.desc: Prevent the container process from gaining additional privileges
commands.tooltip: Prevents the container process from escalating its own privileges (e.g. via setuid binaries).

commands.desc: Expose a host device to the container
commands.tooltip: Passes a host device (like a disk or USB device) directly into the container.

commands.desc: Set ulimit for open file descriptors inside the container
commands.tooltip: Sets limits on the container's resource usage. nofile=1024 limits open file descriptors to 1024.

commands.desc: Use tini as PID 1 to handle zombie processes and signal forwarding
commands.tooltip: Runs tini as PID 1, which properly forwards signals and reaps zombie processes — best practice for containers.

commands.desc: Set the logging driver to json-file (default)
commands.tooltip: Sets the logging driver to json-file, which stores logs as JSON on the host's disk (the default).

commands.desc: Rotate logs at 10 MB, keeping 3 files
commands.tooltip: Configures log rotation: rotate log files when they hit 10MB, keeping 3 rotated copies.

commands.desc: Attach a metadata label to the running container
commands.tooltip: Attaches a metadata label (version=1.0) to the container. Labels are key-value pairs for organization.

commands.desc: Define a health check command for the container
commands.tooltip: Defines a health check command. Docker runs this periodically to see if the container is healthy.

commands.desc: Set health check interval to 30 seconds
commands.tooltip: Sets how often Docker runs the health check — every 30 seconds.

commands.desc: Disable the image's built-in health check
commands.tooltip: Disables the health check that was defined in the image's Dockerfile.

commands.desc: Assign a specific static IP address to a container on a user-defined network
commands.tooltip: Assigns a fixed IP address to the container on a user-defined network.

commands.desc: Force running an image for a specific platform (e.g. ARM64)
commands.tooltip: Runs an image built for a specific CPU architecture, like ARM64 (used on Apple Silicon or Raspberry Pi).

commands.desc: List running containers (modern syntax equivalent to docker ps)
commands.tooltip: Lists running containers using the modern 'docker container' subcommand syntax.

commands.desc: List all containers including stopped ones (modern syntax)
commands.tooltip: Lists all containers (running and stopped) using modern syntax.

commands.desc: Start a stopped container by name or ID
commands.tooltip: Starts a stopped container using its name or ID.

commands.desc: Gracefully stop a running container (sends SIGTERM, then SIGKILL)
commands.tooltip: Stops a container gracefully: sends SIGTERM and waits 10 seconds, then sends SIGKILL if still running.

commands.desc: Stop then start a container
commands.tooltip: Stops then starts a container.

commands.desc: Immediately kill a container with SIGKILL
commands.tooltip: Immediately kills a container with SIGKILL — no graceful shutdown period.

commands.desc: Send a specific signal (e.g. SIGHUP to reload) to a container
commands.tooltip: Sends a specific signal to a container. SIGHUP is often used to trigger a configuration reload.

commands.desc: Suspend all processes in a container (freeze in place)
commands.tooltip: Freezes all processes in a container in place without stopping it.

commands.desc: Resume a paused container
commands.tooltip: Unfreezes a paused container, resuming all its processes.

commands.desc: Remove a stopped container
commands.tooltip: Removes a stopped container.

commands.desc: Force-remove a running container without stopping first
commands.tooltip: Force-removes a running container without stopping it first.

commands.desc: Remove all stopped containers
commands.tooltip: Removes all stopped containers at once.

commands.desc: Remove all stopped containers without confirmation prompt
commands.tooltip: Removes all stopped containers without asking for confirmation. -f means 'force'.

commands.desc: Show detailed JSON metadata for a container
commands.tooltip: Returns detailed JSON about a container: its configuration, state, mounts, network settings, etc.

commands.desc: Extract a specific field from container inspect using Go template
commands.tooltip: Uses a Go template to extract just one field from the inspect JSON. {{.State.Status}} gets the status.

commands.desc: Show running processes inside a container (like ps aux)
commands.tooltip: Lists the processes running inside a container, like running 'ps aux' on the container itself.

commands.desc: Stream live CPU, memory, network, and I/O stats for all containers
commands.tooltip: Shows a live stream of resource statistics (CPU, memory, network, I/O) for all running containers.

commands.desc: Print container stats once without streaming
commands.tooltip: Takes a single snapshot of container stats instead of streaming live updates.

commands.desc: Format container stats output as a custom table
commands.tooltip: Formats the stats output as a custom table with just the columns you want.

commands.desc: List all port mappings for a container
commands.tooltip: Lists all port mappings for a container (which host port maps to which container port).

commands.desc: Rename a container from myapp to newname
commands.tooltip: Renames a container.

commands.desc: Show filesystem changes made inside a container since it started
commands.tooltip: Shows which files inside the container have been added (A), modified (C), or deleted (D) since the image was started.

commands.desc: Create a new image from a container's current state
commands.tooltip: Creates a new Docker image from the current state of a container — saves its changes as a reusable image.

commands.cmd: docker container commit -m 'added config' myapp myimage:v2
commands.desc: Commit container state with a descriptive message
commands.tooltip: Commits container state as a new image with a descriptive message.

commands.desc: Copy a file from container to host
commands.tooltip: Copies a file from inside a container to the host machine.

commands.desc: Copy a file from host into a running container
commands.tooltip: Copies a file from the host into a running container.

commands.desc: Export a container's filesystem as a tar archive
commands.tooltip: Exports the entire container filesystem as a tar archive — a snapshot of everything inside the container.

commands.desc: Attach your terminal to a running container's stdout/stdin
commands.tooltip: Attaches your terminal's stdin/stdout to a running container. Press Ctrl+P Ctrl+Q to detach.

commands.desc: Block until a container stops, then print its exit code
commands.tooltip: Blocks until the container stops and then prints the exit code. Useful for scripting.

commands.desc: Update resource limits on a running container without restarting
commands.tooltip: Updates resource limits on a running container without restarting it.

commands.desc: Change the restart policy of a running container
commands.tooltip: Changes the restart policy for a running container.

commands.desc: List locally available Docker images
commands.tooltip: Lists all Docker images on your system using the modern image subcommand.

commands.desc: List all images including intermediate build layers
commands.tooltip: Lists all images including intermediate build layers.

commands.desc: List only dangling (untagged) images
commands.tooltip: Lists only images that are untagged and not referenced by any other image (dangling).

commands.desc: List images with a custom formatted table output
commands.tooltip: Shows images as a formatted table with custom columns (repository, tag, size).

commands.desc: Download an image from a registry
commands.tooltip: Downloads an image from a registry. Here, nginx:alpine is the Alpine Linux-based nginx image.

commands.desc: Pull all tagged versions of an image
commands.tooltip: Pulls every available tag of the nginx image — all versions.

commands.desc: Push an image to a registry
commands.tooltip: Pushes an image to a registry using the full image name.

commands.desc: Create a new tag pointing to an existing image
commands.tooltip: Creates a new tag for an existing image. The image data is shared; only the tag is new.

commands.desc: Remove an image by tag
commands.tooltip: Removes a specific image by tag.

commands.desc: Force-remove an image even if used by stopped containers
commands.tooltip: Force-removes an image even if stopped containers were created from it.

commands.desc: Remove all dangling (untagged, unreferenced) images
commands.tooltip: Removes only dangling images (untagged, unreferenced).

commands.desc: Remove all images not used by any container
commands.tooltip: Removes all images not currently used by any container — aggressive cleanup.

commands.desc: Remove unused images older than 24 hours
commands.tooltip: Removes unused images older than 24 hours. --filter 'until=24h' limits by age.

commands.desc: Show detailed metadata for an image in JSON format
commands.tooltip: Shows comprehensive metadata for an image: layers, environment variables, exposed ports, etc.

commands.desc: Extract environment variables baked into an image
commands.tooltip: Extracts environment variables baked into an image using a Go template.

commands.desc: Show the layer history of an image (each RUN/COPY/etc.)
commands.tooltip: Shows each layer of an image and what command created it — useful for understanding image size.

commands.desc: Show full untruncated commands for each image layer
commands.tooltip: Shows the full, untruncated commands for each image layer.

commands.desc: Save an image to a tar archive file for offline transfer
commands.tooltip: Saves an image to a tar file for sharing or offline backup.

commands.desc: Load an image from a tar archive back into Docker
commands.tooltip: Imports an image from a tar file that was previously saved with docker image save.

commands.desc: Import a container filesystem tar as a new image
commands.tooltip: Imports a container filesystem tar as a new image — different from docker image load.

commands.desc: Build an image from the Dockerfile in the current directory
commands.tooltip: Builds a Docker image from the Dockerfile in the current directory.

commands.desc: Build using a specific Dockerfile (not default)
commands.tooltip: Builds using a specific named Dockerfile rather than the default.

commands.desc: Build without using the layer cache (forces fresh build)
commands.tooltip: Builds ignoring the cache — every step runs fresh. Slower but ensures up-to-date results.

commands.desc: Always pull the latest base image before building
commands.tooltip: Forces a fresh pull of the base image (FROM in Dockerfile) before building.

commands.desc: Pass a build-time variable into the Dockerfile
commands.tooltip: Passes a build-time argument (NODE_ENV=production) into the Dockerfile's ARG instruction.

commands.desc: Build only up to a named stage in a multi-stage Dockerfile
commands.tooltip: Stops the build at a named stage in a multi-stage Dockerfile instead of running all stages.

commands.desc: Squash all layers into one after build (reduces image size)
commands.tooltip: Merges all image layers into a single layer after building, reducing total image size.

commands.desc: Build a multi-platform image for AMD64 and ARM64
commands.tooltip: Builds images for multiple CPU architectures simultaneously using Docker Buildx.

commands.desc: Pass a build-time secret file without baking it into a layer
commands.tooltip: Passes a secret file to the build without baking its contents into any image layer — secure for API keys.

commands.desc: Forward SSH agent to the build context (for private git repos)
commands.tooltip: Forwards your SSH agent into the build so the Dockerfile can clone private Git repos.

commands.desc: Attach a metadata label to the built image
commands.tooltip: Attaches a metadata label to the built image.

commands.desc: Export build output to a local directory instead of an image
commands.tooltip: Exports the build result to a local directory instead of creating a Docker image.

commands.desc: Show build output in plain text (no spinners, full logs)
commands.tooltip: Shows full build output without progress spinners — useful for CI logs.

commands.desc: Write the built image ID to a file after build
commands.tooltip: Writes the resulting image ID to a file after building. Useful in CI pipelines.

commands.desc: Use a pre-pulled image as a cache source for build layers
commands.tooltip: Uses an already-pulled image as a cache source, allowing cache reuse from a registry.

commands.desc: Print all environment variables set inside a running container
commands.tooltip: Lists all environment variables set inside a running container.

commands.desc: Read a file inside a running container
commands.tooltip: Reads a specific file inside a container by running cat inside it.

commands.desc: Open a shell inside a container as the root user
commands.tooltip: Opens a shell as the root user inside a container — overrides the default user with -u root.

commands.desc: Run a command in a specific working directory inside the container
commands.tooltip: Runs a command in a specific working directory (-w) inside the container.

commands.desc: Run a command in the background inside a container
commands.tooltip: Runs a command in the background inside a container using -d (detached mode).

commands.desc: Run a shell command string inside a container
commands.tooltip: Runs a shell command string inside the container.

commands.desc: Follow (tail) container logs in real time
commands.tooltip: Follows container logs in real time.

commands.desc: Show only the last 100 log lines from a container
commands.tooltip: Shows only the last 100 lines of container logs.

commands.desc: Show logs from the past 1 hour
commands.tooltip: Shows only logs from the past 1 hour.

commands.desc: Show logs before a specific timestamp
commands.tooltip: Shows logs before a specific timestamp.

commands.desc: Show container logs with timestamps prepended
commands.tooltip: Shows logs with timestamps prepended to each line.

commands.desc: Pipe container logs (stdout+stderr) into grep to filter errors
commands.tooltip: Pipes container logs into grep to filter for error messages.

commands.desc: Create a new bridge network named mynet
commands.tooltip: Creates a new bridge network that containers can connect to for isolated communication.

commands.desc: Create an overlay network (for Swarm multi-host communication)
commands.tooltip: Creates an overlay network, which spans multiple Docker Swarm nodes for multi-host communication.

commands.desc: Create a network with a specific subnet
commands.tooltip: Creates a network with a specific IP subnet range.

commands.desc: Create a network with a specific gateway IP
commands.tooltip: Creates a network with a specific gateway IP address.

commands.desc: Restrict container IP allocation to a sub-range of the subnet
commands.tooltip: Restricts the IP addresses that can be assigned to containers to a sub-range of the subnet.

commands.desc: Create an isolated internal network (no external access)
commands.tooltip: Creates a network with no external connectivity — containers can talk to each other but not the internet.

commands.desc: List all Docker networks on the host
commands.tooltip: Lists all Docker networks on the host.

commands.desc: Show detailed info about a network including connected containers
commands.tooltip: Shows details about a network including connected containers and their IP addresses.

commands.desc: Connect a running container to an additional network
commands.tooltip: Connects an already-running container to an additional network.

commands.desc: Connect a container to a network with a specific IP
commands.tooltip: Connects a container to a network and assigns it a specific IP address.

commands.desc: Disconnect a container from a network
commands.tooltip: Disconnects a container from a network without stopping it.

commands.desc: Remove a network (no containers can be connected)
commands.tooltip: Deletes a network (all containers must be disconnected first).

commands.desc: Remove all unused networks
commands.tooltip: Deletes all networks that aren't being used by any container.

commands.desc: Create a named Docker volume
commands.tooltip: Creates a named persistent volume that Docker manages (independent of containers).

commands.desc: Create a volume using the local storage driver
commands.tooltip: Creates a volume using the local driver (the default behavior).

commands.desc: List all Docker volumes on the host
commands.tooltip: Lists all Docker volumes on the host.

commands.desc: List volumes not mounted by any container
commands.tooltip: Lists only volumes that aren't currently mounted by any container.

commands.desc: Show detailed metadata and mountpoint for a volume
commands.tooltip: Shows metadata about a volume, including where Docker stores the data on the host.

commands.desc: Delete a named volume (must not be in use)
commands.tooltip: Deletes a named volume permanently. All data inside is lost.

commands.desc: Remove all unused local volumes
commands.tooltip: Deletes all volumes not currently used by any container.

commands.desc: Remove all unused volumes without confirmation
commands.tooltip: Deletes all unused volumes without asking for confirmation.

commands.desc: Mount a named volume to /app/data inside the container
commands.tooltip: Mounts a named volume at /app/data inside the container — data persists between container restarts.

commands.desc: Bind-mount the current directory into the container at /app
commands.tooltip: Bind-mounts the current directory into the container at /app. Changes on the host are immediately visible inside.

commands.desc: Bind-mount using explicit --mount syntax (more readable)
commands.tooltip: Bind-mounts using the explicit --mount syntax — more verbose but clearer about what's happening.

commands.desc: Mount a named volume using explicit --mount syntax
commands.tooltip: Mounts a named volume using explicit --mount syntax.

commands.desc: Mount a tmpfs (in-memory) volume using --mount syntax
commands.tooltip: Mounts a temporary in-memory filesystem using explicit --mount syntax.

commands.desc: Mount a volume as read-only inside the container
commands.tooltip: Mounts a volume as read-only. The container can read data but cannot modify it.

commands.desc: Show disk usage by images, containers, volumes, and build cache
commands.tooltip: Shows disk usage broken down by images, containers, volumes, and build cache.

commands.desc: Show verbose disk usage with per-item breakdown
commands.tooltip: Shows detailed disk usage with per-item breakdown (each image and container listed separately).

commands.desc: Remove all stopped containers, unused networks, dangling images, and build cache
commands.tooltip: Removes stopped containers, unused networks, dangling images, and build cache.

commands.desc: Remove all unused resources including untagged images and volumes
commands.tooltip: More aggressive: also removes untagged images (not just dangling).

commands.desc: Full cleanup including unused volumes (use with caution)
commands.tooltip: The most aggressive cleanup: also removes volumes. Use with caution — all stored data is deleted.

commands.desc: Remove resources unused for more than 48 hours
commands.tooltip: Removes only resources that haven't been used in the past 48 hours.

commands.desc: Display system-wide Docker info: driver, storage, memory, containers
commands.tooltip: Shows system-wide Docker information: storage driver, logging driver, memory, number of containers, etc.

commands.desc: Stream real-time events from the Docker daemon (start, stop, kill, etc.)
commands.tooltip: Streams real-time events from the Docker daemon as they happen (container starts, stops, image pulls, etc.).

commands.desc: Stream only container-related Docker daemon events
commands.tooltip: Filters the event stream to show only container-related events.

commands.desc: Show Docker events from the past hour
commands.tooltip: Shows Docker events that occurred in the past hour.

commands.desc: Create and start all services defined in compose.yaml
commands.tooltip: Starts all services defined in compose.yaml, creating containers, networks, and volumes as needed.

commands.desc: Start all services in detached (background) mode
commands.tooltip: Starts all compose services in the background.

commands.desc: Rebuild images before starting services
commands.tooltip: Rebuilds service images before starting — ensures you're running the latest code.

commands.desc: Recreate containers even if config hasn't changed
commands.tooltip: Forces Docker to recreate containers even if their configuration hasn't changed.

commands.desc: Start only the web service without starting its dependencies
commands.tooltip: Starts a specific service without starting any of its dependencies.

commands.desc: Start 3 replicas of the web service
commands.tooltip: Starts 3 instances of the web service for load testing or scaling.

commands.desc: Remove containers for services no longer in compose.yaml
commands.tooltip: Also removes containers for services that are in compose but not in the current compose file.

commands.desc: Start services and wait until all health checks pass
commands.tooltip: Starts services and waits until all health checks pass before returning.

commands.desc: Stop and remove containers, networks defined in compose.yaml
commands.tooltip: Stops and removes all containers and networks created by compose.

commands.desc: Stop and remove containers, networks, and volumes
commands.tooltip: Stops and removes containers, networks, and all named volumes.

commands.desc: Stop and remove containers, networks, and all built images
commands.tooltip: Stops and removes containers, networks, and all images that were built for the services.

commands.desc: Also remove orphaned containers not in current compose file
commands.tooltip: Also removes containers that were created by a previous compose version but aren't in the current file.

commands.desc: List containers for the current compose project
commands.tooltip: Lists all containers in the compose project and their status.

commands.desc: List all containers including stopped ones in the project
commands.tooltip: Lists all containers including stopped ones.

commands.desc: Output compose service status as JSON
commands.tooltip: Outputs the service status as JSON, useful for scripting.

commands.desc: View log output from all services
commands.tooltip: Shows the combined log output from all services.

commands.desc: Follow (tail) logs from all services in real time
commands.tooltip: Follows (tails) logs from all services in real time.

commands.desc: Follow logs for a specific service (web)
commands.tooltip: Follows logs from a specific service (web) only.

commands.desc: Show the last 50 lines from a specific service
commands.tooltip: Shows only the last 50 log lines from a service.

commands.desc: Build or rebuild service images
commands.tooltip: Builds or rebuilds service images defined in the compose file.

commands.desc: Rebuild a specific service's image without cache
commands.tooltip: Rebuilds a specific service's image from scratch, ignoring cached layers.

commands.desc: Build all service images in parallel
commands.tooltip: Builds all service images simultaneously instead of one by one.

commands.desc: Pull the latest images for all services
commands.tooltip: Pulls the latest versions of all images used by services.

commands.desc: Pull the latest image for a specific service
commands.tooltip: Pulls the latest image for just the web service.

commands.desc: Push service images to their configured registries
commands.tooltip: Pushes service images to their configured registries.

commands.desc: Start existing stopped containers (does not recreate)
commands.tooltip: Starts containers that already exist but are stopped, without recreating them.

commands.desc: Stop running containers without removing them
commands.tooltip: Stops running containers without removing them. They can be restarted with docker compose start.

commands.desc: Restart all service containers
commands.tooltip: Restarts all service containers.

commands.desc: Restart only the web service container
commands.tooltip: Restarts only the web service container.

commands.desc: Pause all running service containers
commands.tooltip: Pauses all running service containers (freezes their processes).

commands.desc: Unpause all paused service containers
commands.tooltip: Resumes all paused containers.

commands.desc: Open an interactive shell in the running web service
commands.tooltip: Opens an interactive shell in the web service container.

commands.cmd: docker compose exec -u root web bash
commands.desc: Open a shell in the web service as root
commands.tooltip: Opens a shell in the web service as the root user.

commands.desc: Run a shell command inside a compose service container
commands.tooltip: Runs a shell command inside a compose service container.

commands.cmd: docker compose run --rm web python manage.py migrate
commands.desc: Run a one-off command in a new container for a service, then remove it
commands.tooltip: Creates a temporary container for the web service, runs a one-off command, then deletes the container.

commands.cmd: docker compose run --rm -e DEBUG=1 web bash
commands.desc: Run a one-off shell with a custom env var set
commands.tooltip: Runs a one-off container with a custom environment variable, then deletes it.

commands.desc: Run a one-off command without starting dependency services
commands.tooltip: Runs a command in a new container for the web service without starting its dependency services.

commands.desc: Validate and print the merged compose configuration
commands.tooltip: Validates and displays the final merged configuration from all compose files. Useful for debugging.

commands.desc: Validate compose config silently (exit code indicates validity)
commands.tooltip: Validates the compose config silently — exits with 0 if valid, non-zero if there are errors.

commands.desc: Print a list of all service names in the compose file
commands.tooltip: Lists all service names defined in the compose file.

commands.desc: Print a list of all volume names in the compose file
commands.tooltip: Lists all volume names defined in the compose file.

commands.desc: Use a specific compose file (not the default compose.yaml)
commands.tooltip: Uses a specific compose file instead of the default compose.yaml or docker-compose.yml.

commands.desc: Merge multiple compose files (override takes precedence)
commands.tooltip: Merges multiple compose files — settings in the override file take precedence over the base file.

commands.desc: Use a specific .env file for variable substitution
commands.tooltip: Uses a specific .env file for variable substitution, instead of the default .env.

commands.desc: Start services that match the 'debug' profile
commands.tooltip: Starts only services that have a matching profile label, useful for optional or debug services.

commands.desc: Display running processes inside each service container
commands.tooltip: Shows the processes running inside each service container.

commands.desc: Print the host port bound to a service's container port
commands.tooltip: Shows which host port a service's container port is mapped to.

commands.desc: List images used by all service containers
commands.tooltip: Lists all images used by compose service containers.

commands.desc: Send a signal to a service container
commands.tooltip: Sends a signal to a service container. SIGTERM asks it to stop gracefully.

commands.desc: Remove stopped service containers
commands.tooltip: Removes stopped service containers.

commands.desc: Stop and force-remove all service containers
commands.tooltip: Stops all service containers and force-removes them.

commands.desc: Watch source files and auto-sync/rebuild on changes (dev mode)
commands.tooltip: Watches source files for changes and automatically syncs or rebuilds the affected service (Docker Desktop feature).

commands.desc: Preview what compose up would do without executing it
commands.tooltip: Shows what 'docker compose up' would do without actually doing it — a dry run.

commands.desc: Stream real-time events from compose services
commands.tooltip: Streams real-time events from compose services.
