commands.desc: Wait until specified services complete (exit 0)
commands.tooltip: Waits until the specified services have completed and exited with code 0.

commands.desc: Copy a file from a compose service container to the host
commands.tooltip: Copies a file from a compose service container to the host.

commands.desc: List all buildx builder instances
commands.tooltip: Lists all buildx builder instances. Buildx is the advanced Docker build subsystem supporting multi-platform builds.

commands.desc: Create and activate a new buildx builder instance
commands.tooltip: Creates a new buildx builder and makes it the active one. --use sets it as the current builder.

commands.desc: Create a buildx builder using the docker-container driver (supports multi-platform)
commands.tooltip: Creates a buildx builder using the docker-container driver, which runs BuildKit in a container — required for multi-platform builds.

commands.desc: Inspect the current builder and ensure it's running
commands.tooltip: Verifies the current buildx builder is running and bootstraps it if needed.

commands.desc: Switch to a different buildx builder instance
commands.tooltip: Switches to a different buildx builder.

commands.desc: Remove a buildx builder instance
commands.tooltip: Removes a buildx builder instance and its cache.

commands.desc: Stop a buildx builder instance
commands.tooltip: Stops a running buildx builder instance.

commands.desc: Build and push a multi-platform image for AMD64 and ARM64
commands.tooltip: Builds and immediately pushes a multi-platform image (AMD64 + ARM64) to a registry.

commands.desc: Export build cache to a registry for CI reuse
commands.tooltip: Saves the build cache to a registry so CI systems can reuse it on subsequent runs.

commands.desc: Import build cache from a registry
commands.tooltip: Loads build cache from a registry to speed up the current build.

commands.desc: Export the build as an OCI tar archive
commands.tooltip: Exports the built image as an OCI-format tar archive instead of loading it into Docker.

commands.desc: Build multiple targets defined in a docker-bake.hcl or compose file
commands.tooltip: Builds multiple targets defined in a docker-bake.hcl file (Docker's declarative build configuration).

commands.desc: Build and push all targets from a bake file
commands.tooltip: Builds and pushes all targets from a bake file.

commands.desc: Inspect a multi-platform image manifest in a registry
commands.tooltip: Shows the manifest of a multi-platform image in a registry — lists supported platforms.

commands.cmd: docker buildx imagetools create --tag myrepo/myapp:latest myrepo/myapp:amd64 myrepo/myapp:arm64
commands.desc: Create a multi-arch manifest list combining platform-specific images
commands.tooltip: Creates a multi-arch manifest list combining separately built AMD64 and ARM64 images under one tag.

commands.desc: Show build cache disk usage for the current builder
commands.tooltip: Shows how much disk space the current builder's cache is using.

commands.desc: Remove build cache for the current builder
commands.tooltip: Clears the build cache for the current builder.

commands.desc: Remove all build cache without prompting
commands.tooltip: Clears all build cache without asking for confirmation.

commands.desc: Show the installed buildx plugin version
commands.tooltip: Shows the installed version of the Docker Buildx plugin.

commands.desc: Show a quick vulnerability summary for an image
commands.tooltip: Shows a quick summary of vulnerability counts (critical, high, medium, low) in an image.

commands.desc: List all CVEs found in an image
commands.tooltip: Lists all CVEs (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) found in an image's packages.

commands.desc: Show only critical and high severity vulnerabilities
commands.tooltip: Shows only critical and high severity vulnerabilities to focus on the most dangerous issues.

commands.desc: Show only CVEs that have an available fix
commands.tooltip: Shows only vulnerabilities that have patches available — helps you prioritize actionable items.

commands.desc: Show CVEs for a specific package only
commands.tooltip: Filters CVEs to show only those affecting a specific package like OpenSSL.

commands.desc: Exit with code 2 if critical CVEs found (useful in CI)
commands.tooltip: Exits with a non-zero code if critical CVEs are found — useful in CI to fail builds with critical vulnerabilities.

commands.desc: Generate a Software Bill of Materials for an image
commands.tooltip: Generates an SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) — a list of all packages and dependencies in an image.

commands.desc: Output SBOM in SPDX format (industry standard)
commands.tooltip: Outputs the SBOM in SPDX format, a widely adopted industry standard for software supply chain.

commands.desc: Output SBOM in CycloneDX format
commands.tooltip: Outputs the SBOM in CycloneDX format, another industry standard for SBOM.

commands.desc: Save the SBOM to a JSON file
commands.tooltip: Saves the SBOM to a JSON file instead of printing it.

commands.desc: Compare vulnerabilities between two versions of an image
commands.tooltip: Compares the vulnerabilities between two versions of an image to see what changed.

commands.desc: Compare your image against its upstream base image
commands.tooltip: Compares your image's vulnerabilities against its base image to see what you've added.

commands.desc: Show recommended base image updates to reduce vulnerabilities
commands.tooltip: Suggests which base image you should switch to in order to reduce the number of vulnerabilities.

commands.desc: Evaluate image against defined security policies
commands.tooltip: Checks whether an image meets your organization's defined security policies.

commands.desc: Enroll a Docker Hub organization with Scout for continuous monitoring
commands.tooltip: Enrolls a Docker Hub organization with Docker Scout for continuous vulnerability monitoring.

commands.cmd: docker scout repo enable --org myorg myapp
commands.desc: Enable Scout scanning for a specific repository
commands.tooltip: Enables Scout scanning for a specific repository.

commands.desc: List all Scout-enrolled repositories for an organization
commands.tooltip: Lists all repositories enrolled in Docker Scout for an organization.

commands.desc: Clear the local Scout analysis cache
commands.tooltip: Clears the local Scout analysis cache to force fresh analysis.

commands.desc: Show the installed Docker Scout CLI version
commands.tooltip: Shows the installed Docker Scout CLI version.

commands.desc: Authenticate to Docker Hub (prompts for username and password)
commands.tooltip: Logs in to Docker Hub — required before pushing images. Prompts for username and password.

commands.desc: Authenticate to a private container registry
commands.tooltip: Logs in to a private container registry (not Docker Hub).

commands.desc: Log in to a registry non-interactively (avoid in scripts; use stdin)
commands.tooltip: Logs in non-interactively by providing credentials in the command. Avoid in scripts — use stdin instead.

commands.desc: Log out from Docker Hub
commands.tooltip: Logs out from Docker Hub, removing stored credentials.

commands.desc: Log out from a specific registry
commands.tooltip: Logs out from a specific registry.

commands.desc: Search Docker Hub for public images matching 'nginx'
commands.tooltip: Searches Docker Hub for public images. Shows stars, official status, and automated build flag.

commands.desc: Search Docker Hub and filter by minimum star count
commands.tooltip: Searches Docker Hub but only shows images with at least 100 stars — filters out obscure images.

commands.desc: Search Docker Hub for official images only
commands.tooltip: Searches for only official Docker images — maintained by Docker or the software vendor.

commands.desc: Sign an image with Docker Content Trust
commands.tooltip: Signs an image with Docker Content Trust using a cryptographic key for supply chain security.

commands.desc: Show trust data and signatures for an image
commands.tooltip: Shows trust data and digital signatures for a signed image.

commands.desc: Pull an image only if it has a valid content trust signature
commands.tooltip: Enables Docker Content Trust for this pull — refuses to pull the image if it lacks a valid signature.

commands.desc: Initialize the current node as a Swarm manager
commands.tooltip: Initializes the current machine as a Docker Swarm manager. Swarm is Docker's built-in clustering system.

commands.desc: Initialize a Swarm manager with a specific advertised IP
commands.tooltip: Initializes a Swarm manager and specifies which IP address to advertise to worker nodes.

commands.desc: Join a Swarm as a worker node using a join token
commands.tooltip: Joins an existing Swarm as a worker node using the join token.

commands.desc: Print the command needed to add a new worker to the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Prints the command (with token) needed to add a new worker to the Swarm.

commands.desc: Print the command needed to add a new manager to the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Prints the command (with token) needed to add a new manager to the Swarm.

commands.desc: Leave the current Swarm (on a worker node)
commands.tooltip: Removes this node from the Swarm (worker only).

commands.desc: Force a manager node to leave the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Forces a manager node to leave the Swarm — needed when it's the last manager.

commands.desc: Enable autolock to encrypt the Swarm's raft logs at rest
commands.tooltip: Enables Swarm autolock so you must unlock the Swarm manually after a manager restarts.

commands.desc: Unlock a locked Swarm manager after restart
commands.tooltip: Unlocks an autolocked Swarm manager after a restart.

commands.desc: List all nodes in the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Lists all nodes (machines) in the Swarm cluster.

commands.desc: Show details about the current Swarm node in human-readable format
commands.tooltip: Shows detailed information about the current Swarm node in a readable format.

commands.desc: Promote a worker node to manager status
commands.tooltip: Promotes a worker node to manager status, increasing fault tolerance.

commands.desc: Demote a manager node back to worker
commands.tooltip: Demotes a manager node back to a worker.

commands.desc: Remove a node from the Swarm (must be drained first)
commands.tooltip: Removes a node from the Swarm. The node must be drained first.

commands.desc: Drain a node (stop scheduling new tasks on it, migrate existing ones)
commands.tooltip: Drains a node: stops scheduling new tasks on it and migrates existing ones to other nodes. Used before maintenance.

commands.desc: Make a drained node available for task scheduling again
commands.tooltip: Makes a drained node available for scheduling tasks again.

commands.desc: Create a Swarm service running 3 nginx replicas
commands.tooltip: Creates a Swarm service running 3 nginx containers distributed across the cluster.

commands.desc: List all running Swarm services
commands.tooltip: Lists all running Swarm services.

commands.desc: List tasks (containers) running for the web service across all nodes
commands.tooltip: Lists the individual tasks (containers) running across all nodes for the web service.

commands.desc: Show service details in human-readable format
commands.tooltip: Shows service details (image, replicas, port mappings, etc.) in a readable format.

commands.desc: Scale a service to 5 replicas
commands.tooltip: Changes the number of replicas for a service — scales up or down.

commands.desc: Rolling update a service to a new image
commands.tooltip: Updates a service to a new image version using a rolling update (one container at a time).

commands.desc: Update the number of replicas for a running service
commands.tooltip: Explicitly updates the replica count for a running service.

commands.desc: Roll back a service to its previous configuration
commands.tooltip: Rolls back a service to its previous configuration if an update caused problems.

commands.desc: Control rolling update speed: 2 tasks at a time, 10s between batches
commands.tooltip: Controls the rolling update strategy: update 2 tasks at a time, waiting 10 seconds between each batch.

commands.desc: Remove a Swarm service and stop all its tasks
commands.tooltip: Removes a Swarm service and stops all its running containers.

commands.desc: Follow logs from all tasks of a Swarm service
commands.tooltip: Streams live logs from all containers running as part of a Swarm service.

commands.desc: Deploy a multi-service application stack from a compose file to the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Deploys a multi-service application stack from a docker-compose file to the Swarm.

commands.desc: List all deployed Swarm stacks
commands.tooltip: Lists all deployed application stacks on the Swarm.

commands.desc: List services in a deployed stack
commands.tooltip: Lists all services in a specific stack.

commands.desc: List all tasks across all services in a stack
commands.tooltip: Lists all individual tasks across all services in a stack.

commands.desc: Remove an entire deployed stack from the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Removes an entire application stack from the Swarm.

commands.desc: Store a config file in the Swarm (accessible to services)
commands.tooltip: Stores a configuration file in the Swarm's distributed store. Services can read it without baking it into images.

commands.desc: List all configs stored in the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Lists all configs stored in the Swarm.

commands.desc: Show metadata and content of a Swarm config
commands.tooltip: Shows details and content of a specific Swarm config.

commands.desc: Remove a config from the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Removes a config from the Swarm.

commands.desc: Store a secret in the Swarm (encrypted at rest and in transit)
commands.tooltip: Stores a secret in the Swarm — encrypted at rest and in transit. Services access it at /run/secrets/.

commands.desc: List all secrets stored in the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Lists the names of all secrets in the Swarm (values are never shown).

commands.desc: Remove a secret from the Swarm
commands.tooltip: Removes a secret from the Swarm.

commands.desc: List all Docker contexts (local and remote endpoints)
commands.tooltip: Lists all Docker contexts — each context points to a different Docker daemon (local or remote).

commands.desc: Switch to a different Docker context (e.g. remote server)
commands.tooltip: Switches Docker to use a different context, so all commands go to that daemon.

commands.desc: Create a context pointing to a remote Docker host over SSH
commands.tooltip: Creates a new context pointing to a remote Docker host over SSH.

commands.desc: Show details about a Docker context
commands.tooltip: Shows details about a Docker context including the endpoint URL.

commands.desc: Remove a Docker context
commands.tooltip: Removes a Docker context.

commands.desc: Run any docker command against a specific context without switching
commands.tooltip: Runs a docker command against a specific context without permanently switching to it.

commands.desc: Export a context to a file for sharing
commands.tooltip: Exports a context to a file so you can share it with a colleague.

commands.desc: Import a context from an exported file
commands.tooltip: Imports a context from an exported file.

commands.desc: Show Docker client and server (daemon) version information
commands.tooltip: Shows the versions of the Docker client and the server daemon.

commands.desc: Output docker info as JSON (for scripting)
commands.tooltip: Outputs docker info (system configuration) as JSON for scripting.

commands.desc: Extract a container's IP address using Go template format
commands.tooltip: Extracts a container's IP address using a Go template — cleaner than parsing full JSON.

commands.desc: Get container IP across all connected networks
commands.tooltip: Gets the container's IP across all networks it's connected to.

commands.desc: Stream Docker events, filtered to only container death events
commands.tooltip: Streams Docker daemon events, filtered to only show container 'die' (exit) events.

commands.desc: Watch resource usage of all containers in a compact table
commands.tooltip: Watches resource usage for all containers in a formatted table. --all includes stopped containers.

commands.desc: Show processes inside a container with full ps aux output
commands.tooltip: Shows processes inside a container with full 'ps aux' style output.

commands.desc: Copy a log file out of a running or stopped container
commands.tooltip: Copies a log file out of a container to the host for inspection.

commands.desc: Save and compress an image to a gzipped tar archive
commands.tooltip: Saves and compresses a Docker image to a .tar.gz file.

commands.desc: Load a gzip-compressed image archive back into Docker
commands.tooltip: Decompresses and loads an image from a .tar.gz archive back into Docker.

commands.desc: Show the manifest (platforms and digests) for a registry image
commands.tooltip: Shows the image manifest from a registry — lists supported platforms and their digests.

commands.cmd: docker manifest create myapp:latest myapp:amd64 myapp:arm64
commands.desc: Create a multi-arch manifest list from individual platform images
commands.tooltip: Creates a multi-arch manifest list combining separately built images for each platform.

commands.desc: Push a multi-arch manifest list to a registry
commands.tooltip: Pushes the multi-arch manifest list to the registry so users get the right image for their platform.

commands.desc: Print the installed Python version and exit
commands.tooltip: Prints the Python 3 version number and exits. Useful for checking which version is installed.

commands.desc: Execute a Python statement directly from the command line
commands.tooltip: Runs a Python statement directly in the terminal. -c means 'command'.

commands.desc: Run a library module as a script (e.g. http.server, json.tool)
commands.tooltip: Runs a Python library module as a script. -m lets you run things like http.server or json.tool without writing a file.

commands.desc: Run script.py then drop into an interactive REPL with its namespace
commands.tooltip: Runs script.py and then stays in the interactive Python prompt so you can explore the script's variables.

commands.desc: Run with unbuffered stdout/stderr — useful for live log streaming
commands.tooltip: Runs with unbuffered stdout/stderr — output appears immediately instead of being held in a buffer. Essential for live log streaming.

commands.desc: Run with basic optimizations: strips assert statements and __debug__ code
commands.tooltip: Runs with basic optimization: removes assert statements and __debug__ checks. Slightly faster.

commands.desc: Run with extra optimization: also removes docstrings
commands.tooltip: More aggressive optimization: also strips docstrings from functions and classes.

commands.desc: Don't write .pyc bytecache files when importing modules
commands.tooltip: Prevents Python from writing .pyc (compiled bytecode) cache files when importing modules.

commands.desc: Suppress all warnings during execution
commands.tooltip: Hides all Python warnings during execution. -W ignore means 'ignore all warnings'.

commands.desc: Turn all warnings into errors — strict mode for CI
commands.tooltip: Turns all warnings into exceptions (errors). -W error is strict mode — useful in CI to catch deprecation warnings.

commands.desc: Force UTF-8 mode for stdio and file I/O regardless of locale
commands.tooltip: Forces UTF-8 encoding for stdin, stdout, and file I/O, regardless of the system locale setting.

commands.desc: Enable development mode: extra runtime checks and deprecation warnings
commands.tooltip: Enables development mode: extra validation, runtime warnings, and deprecation notices for finding bugs early.

commands.desc: Trace memory allocations for debugging memory leaks
commands.tooltip: Enables memory allocation tracing so you can see what's using memory and find leaks.

commands.desc: Run script under the Python debugger (pdb)
commands.tooltip: Runs a script under pdb, Python's built-in debugger. Lets you step through code line by line.

commands.cmd: python3 -m timeit 'x = [i for i in range(100)]'
commands.desc: Time a Python statement with the timeit module
commands.tooltip: Measures how long a Python statement takes to execute, averaging over many runs.

commands.desc: Pretty-print and validate a JSON file
commands.tooltip: Reads a JSON file, validates it for syntax errors, and prints it formatted with indentation.

commands.desc: Start a simple HTTP file server on port 8080 in the current directory
commands.tooltip: Starts a simple web server serving the current directory's files on port 8080.

commands.desc: Create a virtual environment in the .venv directory
commands.tooltip: Creates a virtual environment (isolated Python environment) in the .venv directory.

commands.desc: Compile script.py to bytecode and report syntax errors
commands.tooltip: Compiles a script to bytecode and reports any syntax errors without running it.

commands.desc: Recursively compile all .py files in the current directory
commands.tooltip: Recursively compiles all .py files in the directory to .pyc bytecode files.

commands.desc: Print site-packages directories and other path info
commands.tooltip: Prints information about where Python looks for modules (site-packages directory etc.).

commands.desc: Pack a Python app directory into a self-contained .pyz executable
commands.tooltip: Bundles a Python app directory into a single .pyz file that can be run as an executable.

commands.desc: Install a package from PyPI
commands.tooltip: Downloads and installs a Python package from PyPI (the Python Package Index).

commands.desc: Install an exact version of a package
commands.tooltip: Installs an exact version of a package. The quotes prevent shell interpretation of ==.

commands.desc: Install a package satisfying a version range
commands.tooltip: Installs a package version that satisfies a range constraint (>= 1.0 but < 2.0).

commands.desc: Install all packages listed in a requirements file
commands.tooltip: Reads a requirements file (list of packages with versions) and installs all of them.

commands.desc: Install the current directory as an editable (development) package
commands.tooltip: Installs the current directory as an editable package — code changes take effect without reinstalling.

commands.desc: Upgrade a package to the latest available version
commands.tooltip: Upgrades a package to the latest version available on PyPI.

commands.desc: Upgrade pip itself to the latest version
commands.tooltip: Upgrades pip itself (the package installer) to the latest version.

commands.desc: Install a package without installing its dependencies
commands.tooltip: Installs a package but skips installing its listed dependencies.

commands.desc: Include pre-release and development versions when searching
commands.tooltip: Includes alpha/beta/release candidate versions in the search — experimental packages.

commands.desc: Install from a specific PyPI-compatible index URL
commands.tooltip: Installs from a specific PyPI-compatible index URL instead of the default pypi.org.

commands.desc: Add an additional index URL to search alongside the main one
commands.tooltip: Adds an extra index URL to search alongside the primary one — useful for private packages.

commands.desc: Search a local directory for package archives to install
commands.tooltip: Looks for package archives in a local directory before checking PyPI.

commands.desc: Skip the pip download cache when installing
commands.tooltip: Skips pip's download cache and downloads fresh from PyPI.

commands.desc: Install package into user site-packages (no sudo needed)
commands.tooltip: Installs into your user's home directory instead of the system Python — no sudo needed.

commands.desc: Install package into a custom target directory
commands.tooltip: Installs into a custom directory instead of the standard site-packages.

commands.desc: Remove an installed package
commands.tooltip: Removes an installed package.

commands.desc: Uninstall all packages in a requirements file without prompting
commands.tooltip: Uninstalls all packages listed in a requirements file without asking for confirmation. -y means 'yes to all'.

commands.desc: List all installed packages and their versions
commands.tooltip: Lists all installed packages and their versions.

commands.desc: List packages that have newer versions available on PyPI
commands.tooltip: Lists packages that have newer versions available on PyPI.

commands.desc: List installed packages in requirements.txt freeze format
commands.tooltip: Lists installed packages in pip freeze format (package==version) instead of a table.

commands.desc: Show metadata for an installed package (version, location, deps)
commands.tooltip: Shows metadata for an installed package: version, author, location, and dependencies.

commands.desc: Output installed packages in requirements.txt format
commands.tooltip: Outputs all installed packages in package==version format, ready to use as a requirements file.

commands.desc: Save current environment's packages to requirements.txt
commands.tooltip: Saves all installed packages and their versions to requirements.txt for reproducibility.

commands.desc: Verify that installed packages have compatible dependencies
commands.tooltip: Checks that all installed packages have mutually compatible dependencies.

commands.desc: Search PyPI for packages matching a term (deprecated in newer pip)
commands.tooltip: Searches PyPI for packages matching a term. Note: this feature was deprecated in newer pip versions.

commands.desc: Download package and its dependencies to a local directory
commands.tooltip: Downloads a package and its dependencies to a local directory without installing them.

commands.desc: Build a wheel for a package and save to ./wheels directory
commands.tooltip: Builds a wheel file (.whl) from a package source and saves it locally. Wheels install faster than source.

commands.desc: Compute the SHA256 hash of a package file for hash-checking mode
commands.tooltip: Calculates the SHA256 hash of a wheel file — used to verify integrity in hash-checking mode.

commands.desc: List all active pip configuration settings
commands.tooltip: Shows all active pip configuration settings from config files and environment variables.

commands.desc: Set the default index URL in pip config permanently
commands.tooltip: Sets a permanent default index URL in the pip config file.

commands.desc: List cached wheel files stored by pip
commands.tooltip: Lists wheel files currently stored in pip's local download cache.

commands.desc: Delete all cached pip download files
commands.tooltip: Deletes all files from pip's download cache to free disk space.

commands.desc: Show pip debug information including platform and Python details
commands.tooltip: Shows debugging information about pip: platform, Python version, paths — useful for troubleshooting.

commands.desc: Create a virtual environment in .venv using the stdlib venv module
commands.tooltip: Creates a virtual environment in .venv using Python's built-in venv module.

commands.desc: Activate a virtual environment (bash/zsh)
commands.tooltip: Activates the .venv virtual environment in bash or zsh. After this, python and pip use the venv.

commands.desc: Deactivate the currently active virtual environment
commands.tooltip: Deactivates the active virtual environment and returns to the system Python.

commands.desc: Upgrade the virtual environment to match a newer Python version
commands.tooltip: Upgrades an existing virtual environment to match a newer Python version while keeping packages.

commands.desc: Create a virtual environment without pip installed
commands.tooltip: Creates a virtual environment but does NOT install pip inside it — smaller footprint.

commands.desc: Copy Python binaries instead of symlinking (useful on Windows)
commands.tooltip: Copies Python binaries into the venv instead of symlinking — useful on Windows or networked filesystems.

commands.desc: Allow the venv to access system-level packages
commands.tooltip: Creates a venv that can also access system-level packages (breaking isolation, but sometimes necessary).

commands.desc: Create a virtual environment using the virtualenv tool (richer than venv)
commands.tooltip: Creates a virtual environment using virtualenv — a third-party tool with more features than the built-in venv.

commands.desc: Create a venv targeting a specific Python version
commands.tooltip: Creates a virtualenv using a specific Python version (useful when multiple versions are installed).

commands.desc: List all Python versions available to install via pyenv
commands.tooltip: Lists all Python versions available to download and install via pyenv.

commands.desc: Download and install Python 3.12.2 via pyenv
commands.tooltip: Downloads and installs Python 3.12.2 via pyenv. pyenv manages multiple Python versions side by side.

commands.desc: Remove a specific Python version installed via pyenv
commands.tooltip: Removes a specific Python version installed by pyenv.

commands.desc: List all Python versions installed via pyenv (with active marker)
commands.tooltip: Lists all Python versions installed via pyenv, marking the currently active one.

commands.desc: Show the currently active Python version and its source
commands.tooltip: Shows which Python version is currently active and whether it's set globally, locally, or for the shell.

commands.desc: Set 3.12.2 as the global default Python version for all shells
commands.tooltip: Sets 3.12.2 as the default Python version for your entire user account.

commands.desc: Pin a project directory to Python 3.11.0 via a .python-version file
commands.tooltip: Creates a .python-version file in the current directory, pinning it to Python 3.11.0.

commands.desc: Set the Python version for the current shell session only
commands.tooltip: Sets the Python version only for the current shell session (overrides global and local settings).

commands.desc: Show the full path of the python binary pyenv would use
commands.tooltip: Shows the full path of the Python binary that pyenv would execute.

commands.desc: Rebuild pyenv shims after installing a new Python version or package with scripts
commands.tooltip: Rebuilds pyenv's shim binaries after installing a new Python version — ensures new tools are found.

commands.desc: Update pyenv itself and all available version definitions
commands.tooltip: Updates pyenv and its database of available Python versions.

commands.desc: Diagnose common pyenv installation problems
commands.tooltip: Runs diagnostics to check pyenv installation for common problems.

commands.desc: Run a command using the pyenv-selected Python explicitly
commands.tooltip: Runs a command using pyenv's Python explicitly, bypassing any other Python in PATH.

commands.desc: Show the installed uv version
commands.tooltip: Shows the uv version. uv is a very fast Python package and project manager written in Rust.

commands.desc: Create a new Python project with a pyproject.toml and .venv
commands.tooltip: Creates a new Python project directory with a pyproject.toml configuration file and a .venv.

commands.tooltip: Runs pytest via uv, ensuring the correct venv is used.

commands.desc: Build the project into a distributable wheel and sdist

commands.tooltip: Uploads built distributions to PyPI.

commands.tooltip: Resolves a requirements.in file (unpinned) to a fully pinned requirements.txt. Like pip-compile.

commands.tooltip: Syncs the environment to exactly match requirements.txt — adds missing packages and removes extras.

commands.tooltip: Pins the project to a specific Python version by writing .python-version.

commands.tooltip: Installs a CLI tool globally in an isolated venv using uv — similar to pipx.

commands.tooltip: Runs a tool without installing it permanently — downloads, runs, and discards. Like npx for Python.

commands.tooltip: Lists all globally installed uv tools.

commands.tooltip: Upgrades a globally installed uv tool to the latest version.

commands.tooltip: Removes a globally installed uv tool.

commands.tooltip: Clears uv's download and build cache.

commands.tooltip: Prints the path to the uv cache directory.

commands.tooltip: Creates a virtual environment at .venv using uv.

commands.desc: Create a venv targeting a specific Python version via uv
commands.tooltip: Creates a venv targeting a specific Python version.

commands.tooltip: Creates a new Python project with src/ layout and pyproject.toml using Poetry.

commands.tooltip: Interactively creates a pyproject.toml in an existing directory.

commands.tooltip: Installs all dependencies from poetry.lock into the Poetry-managed virtual environment.

commands.tooltip: Adds a dependency to pyproject.toml and poetry.lock, installing it immediately.

commands.tooltip: Updates only a specific dependency.

commands.tooltip: Generates/refreshes poetry.lock without installing anything.

commands.tooltip: Refreshes lock file hashes without changing any dependency versions.

commands.tooltip: Lists all packages installed in the Poetry virtual environment.

commands.tooltip: Lists packages that have newer versions available.

commands.tooltip: Runs a command inside the Poetry-managed virtual environment.

commands.tooltip: Opens a new shell with the Poetry virtual environment activated.

commands.tooltip: Builds the package into wheel and sdist distributions for publishing.

commands.tooltip: Uploads the built distributions to PyPI.

commands.tooltip: Publishes to a custom repository (like TestPyPI for testing before a real release).

commands.tooltip: Shows information about the Poetry-managed virtual environment (Python version, path).

commands.tooltip: Lists all virtual environments associated with this project.

commands.desc: Switch the project to use a different Python version
commands.tooltip: Switches the project to use a different Python version.

commands.desc: Delete a specific poetry-managed virtual environment
commands.tooltip: Deletes a specific Poetry virtual environment.

commands.tooltip: Shows all Poetry configuration settings.

commands.tooltip: Configures Poetry to create the .venv inside the project directory instead of a global location.

commands.tooltip: Stores your PyPI API token in Poetry's configuration for publishing.

commands.tooltip: Validates pyproject.toml for correctness and consistency.

commands.tooltip: Exports the lockfile as a pip-compatible requirements.txt without hashes, for use with plain pip.

commands.tooltip: Bumps the patch version number (e.g. 1.0.0 → 1.0.1) in pyproject.toml following semantic versioning.

commands.tooltip: Bumps the minor version number (e.g. 1.0.0 → 1.1.0) in pyproject.toml.

commands.desc: Update poetry itself to the latest version
commands.tooltip: Updates Poetry itself to the latest version.

commands.desc: Discover and run all tests in the current directory (test_*.py files)
commands.tooltip: Discovers and runs all test files matching test_*.py or *_test.py in the current directory.

commands.desc: Run tests in the tests/ directory
commands.tooltip: Runs tests in a specific directory.

commands.desc: Run tests in a specific file
commands.tooltip: Runs tests in a specific test file.

commands.desc: Run a single specific test by node ID
commands.tooltip: Runs a single specific test by its full path (file::class::method). Great for re-running one failing test.

commands.desc: Run tests with verbose output (show each test name)
commands.tooltip: Shows the name of each test as it runs (pass/fail) instead of just dots. -v means 'verbose'.

commands.desc: Run with very verbose output (show diffs on assertion failures)
commands.tooltip: Very verbose: also shows diff output on assertion failures so you can see exactly what mismatched.

commands.desc: Disable stdout capture — allow print() output to appear
commands.tooltip: Disables output capture — lets print() statements show up in the terminal during tests.

commands.desc: Stop after the first failing test
commands.tooltip: Stops after the first test failure instead of running the entire suite. -x means 'exit on first failure'.

commands.desc: Stop after 3 test failures
commands.tooltip: Stops after 3 failures — a balance between seeing all failures and not wading through hundreds.

commands.desc: Run only tests whose name contains 'auth'
commands.tooltip: Runs only tests whose name contains 'auth'. -k filters by test name expression.

commands.desc: Run all tests except those marked or named 'slow'
commands.tooltip: Runs all tests except those with 'slow' in the name.

commands.desc: Run only tests decorated with @pytest.mark.smoke
commands.tooltip: Runs only tests decorated with @pytest.mark.smoke — a way to run a subset of tests by marker.

commands.desc: Re-run only the tests that failed in the last run
commands.tooltip: Re-runs only the tests that failed in the previous test run. --lf means 'last failed'.

commands.desc: Run failed tests first, then the rest
commands.tooltip: Runs failed tests first, then the rest — so you see failures quickly. --ff means 'failed first'.

commands.desc: Run tests in parallel using all available CPU cores (pytest-xdist)
commands.tooltip: Runs tests in parallel across all CPU cores using pytest-xdist. -n auto uses all available cores.

commands.desc: Run tests and report line-level code coverage (pytest-cov)
commands.tooltip: Runs tests and generates a code coverage report showing which lines were executed.

commands.desc: Generate an HTML coverage report in htmlcov/
commands.tooltip: Generates an HTML coverage report in the htmlcov/ directory — open index.html in a browser.

commands.desc: Show shorter tracebacks on failure
commands.tooltip: Shows shorter, less verbose tracebacks on test failures — easier to read.

commands.desc: Suppress all tracebacks (just show pass/fail counts)
commands.tooltip: Hides all tracebacks and just shows the pass/fail summary — minimal output.

commands.desc: Fail any test that takes longer than 30 seconds (pytest-timeout)
commands.tooltip: Marks any test that takes longer than 30 seconds as a failure. Requires pytest-timeout.

commands.desc: Skip a directory when collecting tests
commands.tooltip: Excludes the vendor/ directory when collecting tests.

commands.desc: Show which tests would be collected without running them
commands.tooltip: Shows which tests would be collected and run without actually running them.

commands.desc: Show the 10 slowest tests after the run
commands.tooltip: Shows the 10 slowest tests at the end of the run — useful for finding performance bottlenecks.

commands.desc: Stream logging output to the console at DEBUG level
commands.tooltip: Streams log output to the console at DEBUG level during tests.

commands.desc: Suppress all warning output during test run
commands.tooltip: Suppresses all Python warnings during the test run.

commands.desc: Clear the pytest cache (used by --lf and --ff)
commands.tooltip: Clears pytest's internal cache (used by --lf and --ff) to start fresh.

commands.desc: List all available pytest fixtures
commands.tooltip: Lists all available pytest fixtures (reusable test setup/teardown components).

commands.desc: Collect-only in quiet mode — fast way to list all test IDs
commands.tooltip: Lists all test IDs in quiet mode without running them — fast way to see what's available.

commands.tooltip: Reformats all Python files in the current directory using Black (an opinionated auto-formatter).

commands.desc: Format specific directories with Black
commands.tooltip: Formats specific directories with Black.

commands.desc: Format a single Python file with Black
commands.tooltip: Formats a single Python file.

commands.desc: Check formatting without modifying files (exit 1 if unformatted)
commands.tooltip: Checks if files need formatting without changing them. Exit code 1 means something needs reformatting.

commands.desc: Show what Black would change without modifying files
commands.tooltip: Shows what Black would change as a diff without actually modifying files.

commands.desc: Format with a custom max line length (default is 88)
commands.tooltip: Formats with a different maximum line length than Black's default of 88.

commands.desc: Format targeting Python 3.11 syntax specifically
commands.tooltip: Formats code targeting Python 3.11 syntax — avoids using syntax not available in that version.

commands.tooltip: Reformats files silently, only printing errors.

commands.desc: Skip the slower AST verification step after formatting
commands.tooltip: Skips Black's slow AST (syntax tree) safety check after formatting — faster but slightly riskier.

commands.tooltip: Checks all Python files for style violations using ruff — a very fast Rust-based linter.

commands.desc: Run ruff and auto-fix all automatically fixable issues
commands.tooltip: Lints and automatically fixes all auto-fixable issues in place.

commands.tooltip: Lints using only pycodestyle error (E) and warning (W) rule sets.

commands.tooltip: Lints while ignoring the E501 rule (line too long). You can ignore any rule code this way.

commands.desc: Enable every available ruff rule set
commands.tooltip: Enables every single available ruff rule — maximum strictness.

commands.desc: Watch files and re-lint automatically on changes
commands.tooltip: Watches files and re-lints whenever you save a change — continuous feedback during development.

commands.desc: Output lint results as JSON (useful for CI integrations)
commands.tooltip: Outputs lint results as JSON for integration with CI or other tooling.

commands.desc: Format all Python files with ruff's built-in formatter
commands.tooltip: Formats Python files using ruff's built-in formatter (Black-compatible).

commands.tooltip: Checks formatting without making changes — used in CI to verify formatting.

commands.desc: Show formatting diffs without modifying files
commands.tooltip: Shows formatting diffs without modifying files.

commands.tooltip: Shows documentation and explanation for a specific lint rule code (E501 = line too long).

commands.tooltip: Lists all available linting rules with their descriptions.

commands.desc: Delete the ruff cache directory
commands.tooltip: Deletes the ruff cache directory, forcing a fresh analysis on the next run.

commands.desc: Show what ruff configuration settings are being applied
commands.tooltip: Shows which ruff configuration settings are active (from pyproject.toml or ruff.toml).

commands.desc: Show a summary of violation counts by rule
commands.tooltip: Shows a count of how many times each rule was violated — useful for prioritizing fixes.

commands.tooltip: Runs mypy type checker on the src/ directory. Type checking finds bugs where wrong data types are used.

commands.tooltip: Type-checks a single Python file.

commands.tooltip: Enables all optional strictness flags — the most thorough type checking.

commands.tooltip: Suppresses errors about missing type stubs for third-party packages (like numpy or pandas).

commands.tooltip: Fails on functions that don't have type annotations — enforces that all functions are typed.

commands.tooltip: Also type-checks the bodies of functions that lack annotations.

commands.tooltip: Targets Python 3.11 when checking types — affects which syntax and types are considered valid.

commands.tooltip: Shows the error code (like [arg-type] or [return-value]) for each error — helps when silencing specific errors.

commands.tooltip: Generates an HTML report showing which parts of the code have type annotations.

commands.tooltip: Enables fine-grained incremental caching so unchanged files aren't re-analyzed on the next run.

commands.tooltip: Runs pylint on a single file.

commands.tooltip: Disables a specific pylint warning (C0114 = missing module docstring) to reduce noise.

commands.tooltip: Enables all pylint checkers — maximum coverage.

commands.tooltip: Only reports errors (E codes), ignoring warnings, conventions, and refactoring suggestions.

commands.tooltip: Outputs lint results as JSON for CI integration.

commands.tooltip: Shows the actual source code line below each violation.

commands.tooltip: Ignores specific codes only for specific file patterns — e.g. allow assert in test files.

commands.tooltip: Checks import ordering without changing files — exit code 1 if fixes are needed.

commands.tooltip: Shows what isort would change without modifying files.

commands.tooltip: Sorts imports alphabetically within each import section.

commands.tooltip: Validates that the distributions will be displayed correctly on PyPI's package page.

commands.tooltip: Removes a label from an issue via a DELETE API call.

commands.tooltip: Sets repository topics using a PUT API call.

commands.desc: Make an API call with a custom Accept header
commands.tooltip: Makes an API call with a custom Accept header (needed for some GitHub API features).

commands.desc: Fetch all pages of paginated API results automatically
commands.tooltip: Automatically fetches all pages of a paginated API response and combines them.

commands.desc: Filter API JSON output with a jq expression inline
commands.tooltip: Runs a jq filter expression on the API JSON response to extract specific fields.

commands.cmd: gh api graphql -f query='{ viewer { login } }'
commands.desc: Execute a GraphQL query against the GitHub API
commands.tooltip: Executes a GraphQL query against the GitHub API — GraphQL lets you request exactly the data you need.

commands.desc: Send a JSON request body from a file
commands.tooltip: Sends a JSON request body read from a file instead of specifying fields inline.

commands.desc: Include HTTP response headers in the API output
commands.tooltip: Includes the HTTP response headers in the output alongside the body.

commands.desc: Archive a GitHub repository (make it read-only)
commands.tooltip: Archives a repository, making it read-only on GitHub.

commands.desc: Archive a repository without an interactive confirmation prompt
commands.tooltip: Archives a repository without the interactive confirmation prompt.

commands.desc: Permanently delete a repository without confirmation prompt
commands.tooltip: Permanently deletes a repository without asking for confirmation.

commands.desc: Change a repository's visibility to private
commands.tooltip: Changes a repository's visibility to private.

commands.desc: Disable the Issues feature for a repository
commands.tooltip: Disables the Issues feature for a repository.

commands.desc: Disable the Wiki feature for a repository
commands.tooltip: Disables the Wiki feature for a repository.

commands.desc: Change the default branch of a repository
commands.tooltip: Changes the default branch of a repository to 'main'.

commands.desc: Fork a repository and immediately clone it locally
commands.tooltip: Forks a repository and immediately clones the fork to your local machine.

commands.desc: Fork and add the upstream repo as a remote named 'upstream'
commands.tooltip: Forks and adds the original repository as a remote named 'upstream' for syncing.

commands.desc: Rename the current repository
commands.tooltip: Renames the current repository.

commands.desc: Sync the current fork branch with its upstream source
commands.tooltip: Syncs the current fork branch with its upstream (original) source.

commands.desc: Sync a specific branch from upstream into the fork
commands.tooltip: Syncs a specific branch from the upstream into your fork.

commands.desc: Unarchive a previously archived repository
commands.tooltip: Unarchives a previously archived repository.

commands.desc: List all deploy keys configured on a repository
commands.tooltip: Lists all deploy keys configured on the repository.

commands.desc: Add a deploy key to a repository
commands.tooltip: Adds an SSH public key as a deploy key for CI or deployment access.

commands.desc: Delete a deploy key from a repository by its ID
commands.tooltip: Deletes a deploy key by its numeric ID.

commands.desc: Create a linked development branch for issue #42
commands.tooltip: Creates a new branch linked to issue #42 for easier tracking.

commands.desc: Create and immediately check out the development branch for issue #42
commands.tooltip: Creates and immediately checks out the development branch for an issue.

commands.desc: Edit the title of issue #42
commands.tooltip: Edits the title of issue #42.

commands.desc: Add labels to an issue
commands.tooltip: Adds one or more labels to an issue.

commands.desc: Remove a label from an issue
commands.tooltip: Removes a label from an issue.

commands.desc: Assign an issue to a milestone
commands.tooltip: Assigns an issue to a milestone for release tracking.

commands.desc: Assign yourself to an issue
commands.tooltip: Assigns yourself to an issue using @me as a shorthand.

commands.desc: Lock conversation on an issue with a reason
commands.tooltip: Locks the conversation on an issue with a reason (off-topic, spam, resolved, or too heated).

commands.desc: Pin an issue to the top of the repository's issue list
commands.tooltip: Pins an issue to the top of the repository's issue list for visibility.

commands.desc: Reopen a closed issue
commands.tooltip: Reopens a closed issue.

commands.desc: Transfer an issue to a different repository
commands.tooltip: Transfers an issue to a different repository in the same organization.

commands.desc: Show CI status checks for the current pull request branch
commands.tooltip: Shows the status of all CI checks for the current pull request branch.

commands.desc: Watch CI checks and refresh until all complete
commands.tooltip: Continuously watches CI checks and refreshes until all checks complete.

commands.desc: Close a pull request without merging
commands.tooltip: Closes a pull request without merging it.

commands.desc: Close a PR and delete its head branch
commands.tooltip: Closes a PR and also deletes its head branch.

commands.desc: Post a comment on a pull request
commands.tooltip: Posts a review comment on a pull request. -b is 'body' (the comment text).

commands.desc: Show the unified diff of the current pull request
commands.tooltip: Shows the full unified diff of all changes in the current pull request.

commands.desc: List only the filenames changed in the current PR
commands.tooltip: Lists only the filenames changed in the PR, without showing the diff content.

commands.desc: Edit the title of a pull request
commands.tooltip: Edits the title of a pull request.

commands.desc: Request a code review from a teammate
commands.tooltip: Requests a code review from a specific teammate.

commands.desc: Mark a draft pull request as ready for review
commands.tooltip: Marks a pull request as ready for review (removes the 'Draft' status).

commands.desc: Reopen a closed pull request
commands.tooltip: Reopens a closed pull request.

commands.desc: Submit an approving review on the current PR
commands.tooltip: Submits an approving review on the current PR, signaling you're happy with the changes.

commands.desc: Request changes on the current PR with a comment
commands.tooltip: Submits a 'request changes' review with a comment explaining what needs to be fixed.

commands.desc: Merge the latest base branch changes into the current PR branch
commands.tooltip: Merges the latest base branch commits into the PR branch to bring it up to date.

commands.desc: List recent GitHub Actions workflow runs for the repo
commands.tooltip: Lists recent GitHub Actions workflow runs in the repository.

commands.desc: List runs for a specific workflow file
commands.tooltip: Lists runs for a specific workflow file.

commands.desc: Show the 20 most recent workflow runs
commands.tooltip: Shows only the 20 most recent runs.

commands.desc: Show details and logs summary for a specific run
commands.tooltip: Shows the steps, status, and summary for a specific workflow run.

commands.desc: Stream the full log output of the most recent run
commands.tooltip: Streams the full log output of the most recent workflow run to the terminal.

commands.desc: Show only the log output from failed steps in a run
commands.tooltip: Shows only the log output from failed steps, making it easier to find the cause of failure.

commands.desc: Watch a running workflow and stream live status updates
commands.tooltip: Watches a running workflow and streams live status updates until it completes.

commands.desc: Cancel a workflow run that is in progress
commands.tooltip: Cancels a workflow that's currently in progress.

commands.desc: Re-run all jobs in a failed workflow run
commands.tooltip: Triggers a re-run of all jobs in a failed workflow run — useful when a transient error caused the failure.

commands.desc: Re-run only the failed jobs in a workflow run
commands.tooltip: Re-runs all jobs in a failed workflow run — useful when a transient error caused the failure.

commands.desc: Download a named artifact from a workflow run
commands.tooltip: Re-runs only the failed jobs in a run rather than all jobs, saving time.

commands.desc: Delete a workflow run from the repository history
commands.tooltip: Downloads a named artifact (build output file) from a workflow run to your local machine.

commands.desc: List all workflows in the repository
commands.tooltip: Permanently deletes a workflow run from the repository's run history.

commands.desc: Show details about a specific workflow
commands.tooltip: Lists all workflow files (.yml files in .github/workflows/) in the repository.

commands.desc: Manually trigger a workflow_dispatch workflow
commands.tooltip: Shows the triggers, status, and recent runs for a specific workflow file.

commands.desc: Trigger a workflow on a specific branch or tag
commands.tooltip: Manually triggers a workflow that has a 'workflow_dispatch' trigger defined.

commands.desc: Trigger a workflow and pass an input field
commands.tooltip: Triggers a workflow on a specific branch or tag rather than the default.

commands.desc: Enable a disabled workflow
commands.tooltip: Triggers a workflow and passes an input value defined in the workflow's 'inputs' block.

commands.desc: Disable a workflow without deleting it
commands.tooltip: Enables a workflow that was previously disabled via gh or the GitHub UI.

commands.desc: List all releases for the current repository
commands.tooltip: Disables a workflow so it won't run automatically, without deleting the file.

commands.desc: Show details for a specific release
commands.tooltip: Lists all releases for the current repository with their tag names and dates.

commands.desc: Create a new GitHub release with title and release notes
commands.tooltip: Shows the title, notes, assets, and metadata for a specific release tag.

commands.desc: Create a draft release and attach build artifacts
commands.tooltip: Creates a new GitHub release tied to a version tag, with a title and release notes.

commands.desc: Create a release with auto-generated notes from commits
commands.tooltip: Creates a draft release and attaches build artifact files for review before publishing.

commands.desc: Publish a draft release
commands.tooltip: Creates a release and automatically generates the release notes from merged PRs and commits.

commands.desc: Mark a release as a pre-release
commands.tooltip: Publishes a release that was previously saved as a draft (sets draft=false).

commands.desc: Download all assets from a specific release
commands.tooltip: Marks a release as a pre-release (alpha, beta, rc) rather than a stable release.

commands.desc: Download only assets matching a glob pattern from a release
commands.tooltip: Downloads all attached files (assets) from a specific release to the current directory.

commands.desc: Upload an additional asset to an existing release
commands.tooltip: Downloads only the release assets whose filenames match a glob pattern like *.zip.

commands.desc: Delete a release without a confirmation prompt
commands.tooltip: Uploads a new file as an asset to an already-published release.

commands.desc: Remove a specific asset from a release
commands.tooltip: Deletes a specific release without showing a confirmation prompt.

commands.desc: Set a repository Actions secret (prompts for value)
commands.tooltip: Removes a specific asset file from a release by name.

commands.desc: Set an Actions secret with the value inline
commands.tooltip: Creates or updates a GitHub Actions repository secret (prompts you to type the value securely).

commands.desc: Set a secret scoped to a specific deployment environment
commands.tooltip: Sets a secret with the value provided directly on the command line (useful in scripts).

commands.desc: List all Actions secrets for the repository
commands.tooltip: Sets a secret that is only available to deployments to a specific environment (e.g., production).

commands.desc: Delete an Actions secret from the repository
commands.tooltip: Lists the names of all Actions secrets for the repo (values are never shown for security).

commands.desc: Set a repository-level Actions variable
commands.tooltip: Deletes a named Actions secret from the repository.

commands.desc: List all Actions variables for the repository
commands.tooltip: Sets a repository-level Actions variable (non-secret configuration value visible in logs).

commands.desc: Delete an Actions variable from the repository
commands.tooltip: Deletes an Actions variable from the repository.

commands.desc: Create a new GitHub Codespace for the current repository
commands.tooltip: Creates a new GitHub Codespace — a cloud-based development environment — for the current repo.

commands.desc: Create a Codespace with a specific machine type
commands.tooltip: Creates a Codespace using a specific machine type (e.g., 32GB RAM standard Linux).

commands.desc: List all your active GitHub Codespaces
commands.tooltip: Lists all your active Codespaces with their names, repos, and states.

commands.desc: SSH into a Codespace interactively
commands.tooltip: Opens an SSH connection into a Codespace so you can use its terminal interactively.

commands.desc: Open a Codespace in VS Code (local or browser)
commands.tooltip: Opens a Codespace in VS Code, either the desktop app or the browser version.

commands.desc: Stop a running Codespace to save compute cost
commands.tooltip: Stops a running Codespace to pause billing while preserving its state.

commands.desc: Permanently delete a Codespace
commands.tooltip: Permanently deletes a Codespace and all its data.

commands.desc: List forwarded ports in the current Codespace
commands.tooltip: Lists which ports inside the Codespace are forwarded to your local machine.

commands.desc: Forward port 3000 from a Codespace to localhost
commands.tooltip: Forwards port 3000 inside the Codespace to localhost:3000 so you can open the app in a browser.

commands.desc: View creation and setup logs for a Codespace
commands.tooltip: Shows the creation and setup logs for a Codespace — useful for debugging devcontainer issues.

commands.desc: Rebuild the Codespace container from the devcontainer config
commands.tooltip: Rebuilds the Codespace container from scratch using the devcontainer.json configuration.

commands.desc: Copy a local file into a Codespace
commands.tooltip: Copies a local file into a Codespace's filesystem.

commands.desc: Rename a Codespace for easier identification
commands.tooltip: Renames a Codespace to a friendlier display name for easier identification.

commands.desc: Search GitHub for Go repos about 'cli tool' with >1000 stars
commands.tooltip: Searches GitHub for repositories matching a query with language and star-count filters.

commands.cmd: gh search issues 'memory leak' --repo owner/repo --state open
commands.desc: Search for open issues containing 'memory leak' in a repo
commands.tooltip: Searches for open issues containing a specific phrase within a single repository.

commands.desc: Find open PRs assigned to you across all repos
commands.tooltip: Finds all open pull requests assigned to you (@me) across all repositories.

commands.cmd: gh search commits 'fix null pointer' --repo owner/repo
commands.desc: Search commits by message text in a specific repository
commands.tooltip: Searches commit messages for a specific text string within a repository.

commands.desc: Search for code patterns within a specific repository
commands.tooltip: Searches the code of a repository for a specific pattern (like searching with Ctrl+Shift+F in an IDE).

commands.desc: Create a new public GitHub Gist from a file
commands.tooltip: Creates a new public GitHub Gist — a lightweight way to share a single file or snippet.

commands.desc: Create a public Gist with a description
commands.tooltip: Creates a public Gist with an optional description.

commands.desc: List your GitHub Gists
commands.tooltip: Lists your GitHub Gists with their IDs, descriptions, and file counts.

commands.desc: View the contents of a specific Gist
commands.tooltip: Shows the contents of a specific Gist by its ID.

commands.desc: Print the raw content of a Gist file
commands.tooltip: Prints the raw file content of a Gist without any formatting.

commands.desc: Clone a Gist as a git repository
commands.tooltip: Clones a Gist as a git repository so you can edit and version it locally.

commands.desc: Edit a Gist file in your $EDITOR
commands.tooltip: Opens a Gist file in your default text editor ($EDITOR) for in-place editing.

commands.cmd: gh gist rename GIST_ID old.txt new.sh
commands.desc: Rename a file within a Gist
commands.tooltip: Renames a file within a Gist from old.txt to new.sh.

commands.desc: Delete a Gist
commands.tooltip: Permanently deletes a Gist.

commands.desc: List all labels in the repository with colors and descriptions
commands.tooltip: Lists all issue labels in the repository with their colors and descriptions.

commands.desc: Create a new issue label with a color and description
commands.tooltip: Creates a new label with a custom hex color and description.

commands.desc: Edit an existing label's color or description
commands.tooltip: Updates an existing label's display color or description.

commands.desc: Delete a label from the repository
commands.tooltip: Deletes a label from the repository.

commands.desc: Copy all labels from another repository into this one
commands.tooltip: Copies all labels from another repository into this one — useful for standardizing label sets.

commands.desc: List all GitHub Actions cache entries for the repository
commands.tooltip: Lists all GitHub Actions cache entries — caches store build artifacts between workflow runs to speed them up.

commands.desc: Delete a specific Actions cache entry by key
commands.tooltip: Deletes a specific cache entry by its key to force the next run to rebuild from scratch.

commands.desc: Delete all Actions cache entries for the repository
commands.tooltip: Deletes every cache entry for the repository, freeing up storage.

commands.desc: Create a gh alias: 'gh co 42' runs 'gh pr checkout 42'
commands.tooltip: Creates a short alias so 'gh co 42' runs 'gh pr checkout 42' — saves typing.

commands.desc: List all configured gh aliases
commands.tooltip: Lists all gh command aliases you have defined.

commands.desc: Remove a previously created gh alias
commands.tooltip: Removes a previously created alias.

commands.desc: Import gh aliases from a YAML file
commands.tooltip: Imports multiple aliases at once from a YAML file.

commands.desc: Get the current value of a gh config setting
commands.tooltip: Shows the current value of a gh configuration setting (like 'editor').

commands.desc: Set VS Code as the editor for gh (e.g. PR body editing)
commands.tooltip: Sets VS Code as the editor that gh opens for things like editing PR bodies.

commands.desc: Disable interactive prompts in gh for CI environments
commands.tooltip: Disables interactive prompts in gh — useful for scripts and CI where no human is present.

commands.desc: List all gh configuration settings and their values
commands.tooltip: Lists all gh configuration settings and their current values.

commands.desc: Install a gh CLI extension from a GitHub repository
commands.tooltip: Installs a community-built gh extension (plugin) from a GitHub repository.

commands.desc: List all installed gh extensions
commands.tooltip: Lists all gh extensions you have installed.

commands.desc: Upgrade all installed gh extensions to latest versions
commands.tooltip: Upgrades all installed extensions to their latest versions at once.

commands.desc: Uninstall a gh CLI extension
commands.tooltip: Uninstalls a specific gh extension.

commands.desc: Browse and discover available gh extensions interactively
commands.tooltip: Opens an interactive browser to discover available gh extensions.

commands.desc: List SSH keys associated with your GitHub account
commands.tooltip: Lists all SSH public keys linked to your GitHub account.

commands.desc: Add a new SSH public key to your GitHub account
commands.tooltip: Adds a new SSH public key to your GitHub account with a label (e.g. 'Laptop').

commands.desc: Remove an SSH key from your GitHub account
commands.tooltip: Removes an SSH key from your GitHub account by its numeric ID.

commands.desc: List GPG keys associated with your GitHub account
commands.tooltip: Lists all GPG keys linked to your GitHub account (used for signing commits).

commands.desc: Add a new GPG key to your GitHub account from a file
commands.tooltip: Adds a GPG public key to your GitHub account from a file.

commands.desc: Remove a GPG key from your GitHub account
commands.tooltip: Removes a GPG key from your GitHub account by its key ID.

commands.desc: Show a summary of PRs, issues, and notifications relevant to you
commands.tooltip: Shows a dashboard of open PRs, assigned issues, and notifications relevant to you across all repos.

commands.desc: Install Gemini CLI globally via npm
commands.tooltip: Installs Gemini CLI globally via npm so you can run 'gemini' from any terminal.

commands.desc: Run Gemini CLI without installing it permanently (via npx)
commands.tooltip: Runs Gemini CLI once using npx without permanently installing it — downloads on demand.

commands.desc: Install Gemini CLI via Homebrew on macOS
commands.tooltip: Installs Gemini CLI on macOS using Homebrew package manager.

commands.desc: Update Gemini CLI to the latest version
commands.tooltip: Updates Gemini CLI to the latest released version via npm.

commands.desc: Uninstall Gemini CLI globally
commands.tooltip: Removes the globally installed Gemini CLI package from your system.

commands.desc: Print the installed Gemini CLI version
commands.tooltip: Prints the installed Gemini CLI version number and exits.

commands.desc: Launch the Gemini CLI interactive REPL (defaults to Gemini 2.5 Pro)
commands.tooltip: Starts the Gemini CLI interactive chat session using the default model (Gemini 2.5 Pro).

commands.desc: Start Gemini CLI using the Gemini 2.0 Flash model
commands.tooltip: Starts Gemini CLI and uses the Gemini 2.0 Flash model — faster and cheaper than Pro.

commands.desc: Start with the latest Gemini 2.5 Pro model (short flag)
commands.tooltip: Starts Gemini CLI using the latest Gemini 2.5 Pro model via the short -m flag.

commands.desc: Start in sandbox mode — code execution isolated in Docker/Podman container
commands.tooltip: Starts Gemini CLI in sandbox mode — all code execution runs inside an isolated Docker/Podman container for safety.

commands.desc: Start in sandbox mode using the short flag
commands.tooltip: Same as --sandbox but using the shorter -s flag.

commands.desc: Start with debug logging enabled — shows API calls and token counts
commands.tooltip: Starts Gemini CLI with debug logging so you can see the API calls, token counts, and internal decisions.

commands.desc: Start with debug logging (short flag)
commands.tooltip: Same as --debug but using the shorter -d flag.

commands.desc: Auto-approve all tool calls without prompting (use with care)
commands.tooltip: Auto-approves all tool calls (file writes, shell commands) without prompting. Use carefully — Gemini will execute actions without asking.

commands.desc: Enable checkpointing — save conversation state to resume later
commands.tooltip: Enables session checkpointing so you can save and resume long agentic conversations later.

commands.desc: Enable telemetry data collection for this session
commands.tooltip: Opts in to sending telemetry (usage data) to Google for this session.

commands.desc: Disable telemetry for this session
commands.tooltip: Opts out of telemetry data collection for this session.

commands.desc: Run a single prompt non-interactively and exit
commands.tooltip: Runs a single prompt non-interactively and exits — useful for scripting or one-off questions.

commands.desc: Pipe file content as stdin for a non-interactive prompt
commands.tooltip: Pipes a file's contents as stdin and passes a prompt — lets you ask Gemini about a file without typing its path.

commands.desc: Pass a prompt via stdin pipe in headless mode
commands.tooltip: Pipes a prompt in headless (non-interactive) mode via stdin.

commands.desc: Return the model response as structured JSON
commands.tooltip: Returns Gemini's response as structured JSON instead of plain text — useful for parsing in scripts.

commands.desc: Non-interactive prompt with a file as context via @ syntax
commands.tooltip: Passes a file as context using the @ syntax and asks a question about it in a single non-interactive call.

commands.cmd: cat error.log | gemini -p 'Diagnose this error log'
commands.desc: Pipe a log file into Gemini CLI for analysis
commands.tooltip: Pipes a log file into Gemini CLI so it can analyze errors and suggest causes.

commands.desc: Pipe source code in, redirect refactored output to a file
commands.tooltip: Pipes Python source code into Gemini and redirects the refactored output to a new file.

commands.desc: Inject the contents of filename.py into the current prompt as context
commands.tooltip: Inside the Gemini REPL, types @filename.py to inject a file's contents into the current prompt as context.

commands.desc: Include the entire src/ directory tree as context for the prompt
commands.tooltip: Inside the Gemini REPL, types @src/ to include the entire directory tree as context for the next prompt.

commands.desc: Reference a file and ask a question about it inline
commands.tooltip: References a file inline with @ and asks a question about it in the same message.

commands.cmd: @package.json What are all the dev dependencies?
commands.desc: Point Gemini at a specific file and ask a targeted question
commands.tooltip: Points Gemini at a specific file and asks a targeted question about its contents.

commands.cmd: @src/ @tests/ Write tests for any uncovered functions
commands.desc: Include multiple paths as context in one prompt
commands.tooltip: References two directories at once as context, then asks Gemini to write tests for uncovered code.

commands.desc: Fetch and include a URL as context using @ syntax
commands.tooltip: Fetches a web page via URL using @ syntax and includes its text content as context.

commands.desc: Run a shell command directly from within the Gemini CLI REPL
commands.tooltip: Runs a shell command directly from inside the Gemini REPL using the ! prefix — like ls -la.

commands.desc: Check git status without leaving the Gemini CLI session
commands.tooltip: Checks git status without leaving the Gemini CLI session.

commands.desc: Execute npm test from inside the Gemini interactive session
commands.tooltip: Runs npm test from within the interactive Gemini session.

commands.desc: Print a file to the terminal from within the Gemini REPL
commands.tooltip: Prints a file to the terminal without leaving the Gemini REPL.

commands.desc: Show all available slash commands and their descriptions in the Gemini CLI
commands.tooltip: Shows all available slash commands and their descriptions inside the Gemini CLI.

commands.desc: Clear the current conversation history to start fresh context
commands.tooltip: Clears the conversation history so Gemini starts fresh with no prior context.

commands.desc: Exit the Gemini CLI interactive session
commands.tooltip: Exits and closes the Gemini CLI interactive session.

commands.desc: Display Gemini CLI version, model, and session information
commands.tooltip: Displays Gemini CLI version, current model, and session info.

commands.desc: Open a pre-filled GitHub issue to report a Gemini CLI bug
commands.tooltip: Opens a pre-filled GitHub issue page to report a bug in Gemini CLI.

commands.desc: Interactively switch the Gemini CLI color theme
commands.tooltip: Opens an interactive color theme picker to change the CLI's appearance.

commands.desc: Switch to the dark color theme
commands.tooltip: Switches Gemini CLI to the dark color theme.

commands.desc: Switch to the light color theme
commands.tooltip: Switches Gemini CLI to the light color theme.

commands.desc: Show token usage statistics for the current session
commands.tooltip: Shows how many tokens the current session has consumed — tokens are the unit of AI processing cost.

commands.desc: Show the current authentication method and switch between login and API key
commands.tooltip: Shows your current authentication method (Google login or API key) and lets you switch.

commands.desc: Open the current prompt or last response in your default $EDITOR
commands.tooltip: Opens the current prompt or last AI response in your system's default text editor ($EDITOR).

commands.desc: Display the privacy policy and manage data-sharing settings
commands.tooltip: Displays the privacy policy and lets you manage data-sharing settings.

commands.desc: Open the Gemini CLI documentation in your default browser
commands.tooltip: Opens the Gemini CLI documentation website in your default browser.

commands.desc: List all built-in and MCP tools available in the current session
commands.tooltip: Lists all available tools (file read, shell, web fetch, etc.) that Gemini can call in this session.

commands.desc: List all enabled Gemini CLI extensions
commands.tooltip: Lists all enabled Gemini CLI extensions.

commands.desc: List connected MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and their tools
commands.tooltip: Lists the MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers connected to this session and the tools they provide. MCP lets external servers give Gemini new capabilities.

commands.desc: Display the current persistent memory that Gemini CLI carries across sessions
commands.tooltip: Shows the persistent memory notes that Gemini CLI remembers across sessions.

commands.cmd: /memory add 'Always use TypeScript with strict mode'
commands.desc: Add a persistent instruction to Gemini CLI's memory
commands.tooltip: Adds a persistent instruction to Gemini CLI's memory — e.g. 'Always use TypeScript with strict mode'.

commands.desc: Reload memory from the GEMINI.md file without restarting the session
commands.tooltip: Reloads the memory from the GEMINI.md file without restarting the session.

commands.desc: Save the current conversation to a named chat history file
commands.tooltip: Saves the current conversation to a named file so you can resume it later.

commands.desc: List all saved chat sessions
commands.tooltip: Lists all saved chat sessions by name.

commands.desc: Resume a previously saved chat session
commands.tooltip: Resumes a previously saved chat session, restoring the full conversation history.

commands.desc: Summarize the current conversation to reduce token usage while preserving context
commands.tooltip: Summarizes and compresses the current conversation to reduce token usage, while keeping essential context.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini reads a single local file and includes its content in context
commands.tooltip: ReadFile is a built-in tool Gemini uses automatically to read a local file and include its content when answering.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini reads a directory listing (non-recursive) to understand structure
commands.tooltip: ReadFolder is a built-in tool Gemini uses to list files in a directory (non-recursive) to understand project structure.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini reads multiple files matching a glob pattern simultaneously
commands.tooltip: ReadManyFiles is a built-in tool that lets Gemini read multiple files matching a glob pattern simultaneously.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini writes content to a file, creating it or overwriting it
commands.tooltip: WriteFile is a built-in tool Gemini uses to create or overwrite a file — it will ask your permission first unless --yolo is set.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini makes targeted edits to an existing file using diff-like patches
commands.tooltip: Edit is a built-in tool Gemini uses to make targeted changes to an existing file, like a smart find-and-replace.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini executes a shell command and captures stdout/stderr
commands.tooltip: Shell is a built-in tool that lets Gemini run terminal commands to gather information or perform tasks.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini fetches a URL and returns its text content as context
commands.tooltip: WebFetch is a built-in tool that lets Gemini retrieve the text content of a URL and use it as context.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini searches for a regex pattern across files in the workspace
commands.tooltip: SearchText is a built-in tool Gemini uses to search for a regex pattern across files in your project.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini finds files by name or glob pattern in the project directory
commands.tooltip: FindFiles is a built-in tool Gemini uses to locate files by name or glob pattern in the project.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini performs a live Google search and incorporates results
commands.tooltip: GoogleSearch is a built-in tool that lets Gemini perform a live Google search and incorporate up-to-date results.

commands.desc: Built-in tool: Gemini saves a note to memory that persists across sessions
commands.tooltip: SaveMemory is a built-in tool Gemini uses to save a persistent note that will be remembered in future sessions.

commands.desc: Create a GEMINI.md project context file for Gemini CLI to auto-load
commands.tooltip: Creates a GEMINI.md file in your project that Gemini CLI automatically reads at startup for project-specific context.

commands.cmd: echo '# Project: My App\nStack: FastAPI + React' >> GEMINI.md
commands.desc: Add project-level context to GEMINI.md that Gemini CLI reads at startup
commands.tooltip: Adds project-specific context (like stack and architecture notes) to GEMINI.md that Gemini loads every session.

commands.desc: View your global (user-level) GEMINI.md persistent memory file
commands.tooltip: Views your global GEMINI.md file — this is the user-level persistent memory Gemini CLI reads at startup.

commands.desc: Add a persistent global instruction to your user-level Gemini memory
commands.tooltip: Appends a global instruction (like 'Always respond concisely') to your personal Gemini memory file.

commands.desc: View the Gemini CLI settings file including MCP server configurations
commands.tooltip: Views the Gemini CLI settings file where you can configure the model, theme, MCP servers, and more.

commands.cmd: # Add MCP server to settings.json under 'mcpServers' key
commands.desc: Register an MCP server so Gemini CLI can call its tools (edit settings.json directly)
commands.tooltip: Registers an MCP server in settings.json — MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets external services give Gemini new tools.
